name: computer_use strategy (Playwright GUI drive) with safety gates
user_story: As an operator, I want AutoClaw to drive a stalled IDE's chat box as a last resort so that GUI-only agents can still be revived — but only when it's safe.
expected_behavior: Gate 1: agent health.state must be 'stalled' else skip. Gate 2: human must be idle (idle probe) else skip. Then resolves a per-IDE script, screenshots before+after, runs declarative steps, audits each action; any step failure → screenshot + 'failed'. Skips cleanly when @playwright/test is absent.
gaps: createPlaywrightDriver is a lazy stub: even when @playwright/test IS installed, focusWindow/click/type/press just return `available` and screenshot() returns false — no real Electron/CDP session is opened (TODO(playwright-dep) at computerUse.ts:70-82). Real GUI automation is unbuilt; the operator-side integration is explicitly left as a stub.
notes: The strategy logic + gates + audit are complete and tested; the actual driver backend is a no-op placeholder.

name: Fleet watch loop (revive stalled agents on a tick)
user_story: As an operator, I want a watcher that periodically reads LMD health and runs the keep-alive chain on every stalled agent so that the whole fleet self-revives.
expected_behavior: FleetWatcher.tick() reads the health grid, filters state==='stalled', and runs StrategyChain per agent, logging each chain result to keepalive log. CLI entrypoint supports --once / interval; toggle command + status-bar text are exported.
gaps: fleet-watch is a standalone CLI (require.main===module); watchFleetCommand / 'AutoClaw: Watch Fleet' is exported but NOT registered in package.json contributes.commands nor wired in extension.ts — no in-editor entry point. The chain it builds passes no runner seam, so 'runner' strategy always skips. | fleet-watch.test.ts not in CI test:unit list.
notes: fleet-watch.ts lives in src/cli (outside the keepalive dir) but is the sole consumer of StrategyChain; included for completeness of the revive feature.

name: Persona provider resolver (synthetic + real LlmRegistry routing)
user_story: As a persona, I want my provider ref resolved to either a deterministic test provider or a real local/mesh model so that the same dispatch code works in tests and production.
expected_behavior: resolveProvider routes 'inline' and 'claude-code-runner' to synthetic providers, and 'ollama:'/'zippymesh:'/'lmstudio:' refs through a lazy LlmRegistry singleton (intent inferred from persona id). Without a workspaceRoot, real refs synthesize a failure to exercise the fallback chain. Unknown ref → internal error.
gaps: 'claude-code-runner' provider is still a synthetic placeholder returning a canned string (deferred to v3.2+) — the real Claude Code shell-out in src/runners/claude-code.ts is not invoked from the persona path. File name is 'provider-stub' though it now also routes real refs.
notes: Phase B S1 integrated real ollama/zippymesh/lmstudio refs; the cloud-runner fallback remains a stub.

name: Persona slash-command (/persona <id> "<prompt>")
user_story: As a user, I want a /persona command in VS Code to invoke a persona against a prompt so that I can run a specialized role interactively.
expected_behavior: registerPersonaCommand registers 'autoclaw.persona', parses '<id> <prompt>', dispatches via PersonaLoader, and shows the response (or error, with a '(using fallback provider)' prefix). No-ops when vscode/workspace unavailable.
gaps: registerPersonaCommand is never called from extension.ts (grep shows only its own export + spec comment), and 'autoclaw.persona' is absent from package.json contributes.commands — so the slash command is unreachable by end users. No test covers command.ts. | Persona skill (Skill tool) handles /persona doc-writer etc. separately; this VS Code command path is orphaned.
notes: The loader/dispatch core is wired-testable, but the user-facing command is unregistered.

name: Pre-push branch conflict detection
user_story: As a parallel-agent operator, I want a warning when two agent branches touch the same files before a push so that I avoid the classic merge-race.
expected_behavior: detectBranchConflicts diffs a branch and each peer against a resolved base ref (origin/main→main→master→empty-tree) with git diff --name-only and reports overlapping files + human warnings. Read-only; never writes/pushes. git runner injectable.
gaps: Module is self-contained and tested but has no caller in production code (no orchestrator/extension/CLI invocation found) — the doc comment says 'the orchestrator / a git pre-push hook calls it' but no such wiring exists in this scope. conflictDetection.test.ts also not in CI test:unit list.
notes: Pure computation is complete; the integration point is unbuilt.

name: runner strategy (headless re-kick seam)
user_story: As an operator, I want a stalled agent re-kicked via its registered headless runner so that the cheapest, GUI-free wake is tried first.
expected_behavior: runnerStrategy delegates to an injected RunnerRekick seam; findRunner===null → 'skipped', dispatchRekick success → 'success', throw → 'failed'. Default seam is a no-op returning 'no_runner'.
gaps: StrategyChain default uses the no-op noRunner seam; no production wiring to the real src/runners RunnerRegistry is present in this scope — only fleet-watch CLI constructs the chain and it too passes no runner seam by default.
notes: Real RunnerRegistry lives in src/runners; the seam is deliberately injected.

name: cli strategy (configured shell wake command)
user_story: As an agent author, I want to set a shell command that wakes my agent so that AutoClaw can re-kick agents that respond to a CLI poke.
expected_behavior: cliStrategy runs config.cliCommand via cmd/sh (30s default timeout, windowsHide); empty command → 'skipped'; exit 0 → 'success'; non-zero/throw → 'failed'. Output truncated to 200 chars.
notes: exec seam is injectable for tests.

name: Computer-use audit log + screenshots
user_story: As an operator, I want every GUI action and a before/after screenshot recorded so that I can audit exactly what the automation did.
expected_behavior: logComputerUseAction appends JSONL to .autoclaw/runtime/computer-use-log/actions.jsonl; screenshotPath builds <ts>-<agent>-<phase>.png paths. Best-effort, never throws.
entry_points: logComputerUseAction(workspaceRoot, action) | screenshotPath(workspaceRoot, agentId, phase) | computerUseLogDir(workspaceRoot)
gaps: Screenshots are never actually written because the stub driver's screenshot() returns false; the log records 'screenshot unavailable' rather than a real PNG until a real driver lands.
notes: Covered via keepalive-computerUse.test.ts (not in CI list).

name: Fleet HALT kill switch (file-based)
user_story: As an operator, I want a single kill switch that pauses all auto-dispatch and trigger hooks so that I can stop the fleet instantly from any shell or the editor.
expected_behavior: Presence of .autoclaw/orchestrator/HALT == halted. setFleetHalted writes who/when/why JSON on engage, unlinks on release. Wired to commands 'AutoClaw: Halt Fleet' / 'Resume Fleet'; also consumed by budget ceiling and the orchestrator loop.
entry_points: autoclaw.fleet.halt | autoclaw.fleet.resume | setFleetHalted(root, halted, reason) | isFleetHalted(root)
notes: Both commands ARE in package.json + registered in extension.ts. budget/ceiling.ts auto-halts on budget breach.

name: Hook executor + audit (dispatch / notify / launch_skill / spawn_runner / relay)
user_story: As an operator, I want fired hooks to perform a real action and have every firing/suppression audited so that auto-wakes are traceable.
expected_behavior: executeHook runs the rule's action via injected deps; every outcome appends to comms/hooks/audit.jsonl and mirrors to comms-log. Missing dep or empty required target → audited hook_error (never throws into watcher). 'dispatch' builds a low-priority WorkPackage and calls orchestratorLoop.dispatchWork (gated/journaled).
notes: HKS-4/5 actions (launch_skill, spawn_runner, relay) marked done in BACKLOG and wired in extension.ts (launchSkill copies prompt to clipboard; spawn_runner validates against BUILTIN_RUNNER_IDS; relay rides CloudRelay, inert unless consented).

name: Human-idle detector (computer-use safety gate)
user_story: As a developer, I don't want AutoClaw hijacking my cursor while I'm typing, so GUI automation must confirm I've been idle first.
expected_behavior: detectIdle probes OS idle time: Windows GetLastInputInfo via inline C#/PowerShell, macOS ioreg HIDIdleTime, Linux xprintidle. Unknown → isIdle:false (fail-safe). Default threshold 3 minutes.
notes: Linux path depends on xprintidle being installed; absent → unknown → treated as active. Tested via computerUse idle-probe injection.

name: Keep-alive config loader from scope.json
user_story: As an agent author, I want to declare keepalive_strategy / keepalive_cli_command / playwright_script in my scope.json so that the chain knows how to wake me.
expected_behavior: loadKeepaliveConfig reads .autoclaw/orchestrator/agents/<id>/scope.json; missing file / bad JSON / missing fields all degrade to DEFAULT_KEEPALIVE_CHAIN. Returns agentId, strategy, cliCommand, playwrightScript, ideLabel.
notes: Covered indirectly via strategyChain tests (not in CI list).

name: Keep-alive strategy chain (runner → cli → computer_use → toast)
user_story: As an orchestrator operator, I want a stalled agent automatically re-kicked through a priority fallback chain so that work resumes without me babysitting every window.
expected_behavior: StrategyChain.run() tries each strategy in the agent's declared order; a 'skipped' result advances to the next, only 'success' stops the chain, and exhaustion yields ok:false. No LLM calls — pure orchestration.
entry_points: StrategyChain (exported from src/keepalive) | StrategyChain.run(config, health, prompt) | loadKeepaliveConfig(workspaceRoot, agentId)
notes: keepalive-strategyChain.test.ts (10 tests) exists but is NOT in package.json test:unit CI file list — it does not run in CI.

name: Non-message hook event builders + in-process bus
user_story: As a subsystem author, I want to emit consensus/autobuild_fail/stall events into the hook engine without import cycles so that those sources can trigger wakes.
expected_behavior: hookEvents.ts builds heartbeat_stall/claim_stale/consensus/autobuild_fail events (pure, deterministic with injected now). hookBus.ts is a leaf emitter: registerHookHandler / emitHookEvent; handler throws are swallowed so an emit never breaks consensus or autobuild.
entry_points: buildHeartbeatStallEvents | buildClaimStaleEvents | buildConsensusEvent | buildAutobuildFailEvent | registerHookHandler / emitHookEvent
notes: Bus binds handlers per workspace root for multi-root correctness.

name: Per-IDE computer-use scripts registry (Kilo Code, Cursor)
user_story: As an integrator, I want pluggable per-IDE submission scripts so that new IDEs lacking a headless path can be driven by id.
expected_behavior: resolveScript(id) maps a playwright_script id to an IdeComputerUseScript (focus/click/type/press/wait steps + window title match). Two ships: 'kilocode-chat-submit', 'cursor-chat-submit'. Unknown id → null → strategy skips. Scripts contain zero Playwright imports.
notes: Only 2 IDEs shipped. Selectors are best-effort and unverified against live DOM since the driver is a stub.

name: Per-persona bi-temporal memory (sharded tiers + promotion + digest)
user_story: As a persona, I want my lessons to accumulate across sessions, age scratch→recall→archive, and surface a human digest so that I get smarter per project over time.
expected_behavior: personas.ts manages scratch/recall/archive lessons.jsonl + lessons.md digest. appendLesson/readAllLessons; promoteLessons ages entries (promoteAfter 2, archiveAfter 8 sessions, drops superseded); writeDigest writes last-N newest-first.
entry_points: appendLesson(root, entry) | promoteLessons(root, config) | writeDigest(root, persona, limit) | readAllLessons(root)
notes: Lives under src/memory (re-exported via src/memory/index.ts) but is persona-specific. personaMemory.test.ts (12 tests) IS in CI list.

name: Persona dispatch with provider fallback chain + ledger
user_story: As a user, I want to run a persona against a prompt and have it fall back to a secondary provider on failure so that a single unreachable model doesn't block the role.
expected_behavior: PersonaLoader.dispatch builds [preferred, fallback], tries each via resolveProvider; success appends a persona_dispatch row to comms-log.jsonl. tool_denied stops the chain and writes a finding_report to shared inbox. Unknown persona → structured error listing available ids.
notes: Cost ledger here is a comms-log JSONL append, distinct from the llm cost ledger.

name: Persona global-memory mirror with secret/privacy gating
user_story: As a security-conscious user, I want a persona's cross-project lessons mirrored to ~/.autoclaw only when they're privacy-cleared so that secrets and project-only facts never leak.
expected_behavior: mirrorToGlobal copies only entries passing isPromotableToGlobal: 'project' never leaves; a secret-scan (token prefixes, Bearer, key=val, PRIVATE KEY, internal host:port, private endpoints) blocks any entry regardless of label. Idempotent by subject; marks mirrored entries 'global'.
notes: Enforces the steering doc's 'never promote secrets/proprietary names' rule.

name: Persona profile loader (SKILL.md frontmatter → PersonaProfile)
user_story: As the orchestrator, I want to load a persona's profile (mission, triggers, tools, provider chain, memory roots) from its SKILL.md so that I can instantiate a specialized role per project.
expected_behavior: PersonaLoader.list()/load() scan skills/<id>/SKILL.md, parse shallow frontmatter, and buildProfile() maps fields to PersonaProfile with local-first provider defaults (ollama:llama3.1:70b → claude-code-runner fallback) and project + global memory roots. Profiles cached.
notes: personas-loader.test.ts (13 tests) IS in CI list. successCriteria is hardcoded to [] in buildProfile — the typed Given/When/Then field is never populated from frontmatter.

name: toast strategy + 'Awaiting You' handoff
user_story: As an operator, I want a clear OS notification and panel entry when automation can't revive an agent so that I know to step in manually.
expected_behavior: toastStrategy fires an OS notification (Win NotifyIcon balloon / mac osascript / Linux notify-send), pushes a VS Code warning via injected bridge, and appends an entry to .autoclaw/runtime/awaiting-you.jsonl. Always reports 'success' (human handoff IS success).
notes: Fleet panel is expected to tail awaiting-you.jsonl; that consumer is outside this scope.

name: Trigger-hook rule engine (hooks.yaml load + pure matcher)
user_story: As an operator, I want event-driven YAML rules that wake agents on fleet events so that reviews/stalls/consensus auto-route without manual nudging.
expected_behavior: parseHooksYaml parses a flat YAML subset (no dep), dropping invalid rules. matchHooks filters by on/equality/_gte/_lte, applies per-rule cooldown (default 300s) and global cap (30/hr), respects HALT, and ignores via_hook events (no self-amplification). loadHookRules returns [] when hooks.yaml absent (zero-config).
entry_points: parseHooksYaml(text, onWarn) | matchHooks(rules, event, state, now, max) | loadHookRules(workspaceRoot) | .autoclaw/orchestrator/hooks.yaml
notes: triggerHooks.test.ts (47 tests) IS in CI list. Precedence: HALT > cooldown > cap > fire.

name: Trigger-hooks runtime (InboxWatcher + stall tick + in-proc bus)
user_story: As an operator, I want hooks to fire automatically off inbox messages, heartbeat/claim staleness, consensus and autobuild events so that the fleet reacts in real time.
expected_behavior: startTriggerHooksRuntime watches the comms tree; zero rules → inert no-op handle. Serializes message/bus/tick handling through one queue; refreshes HALT from disk each event; starts a stall/claim tick only when a rule listens for it (default 60s, 600s staleness floor). Wired in extension.ts activate().
notes: Dispatch target coerced to VendorKind union ('other' fallback). Runtime is wired into extension activation.

name: Hosted (paid) relay with server-side entitlement gate (AF-10b)
user_story: As the vendor, I want a server-side per-token subscription/tier check at the relay so that the hosted relay is a paid feature while the OSS client stays open and unmetered.
expected_behavior: Per docs/specs/relay-entitlement.spec.md: before accepting heartbeat/inbox/flush, the hosted relay would authenticate the token, resolve a subscription (free/pro/enterprise), enforce tier + max_machines + retention, and return 402/429 when over limit.
entry_points: (intended) entitlement check at resolveAccount in a hosted variant
gaps: No tier/subscription/402/429/max_machines logic exists anywhere in src/relay-server — only comments naming the future seam. BACKLOG AF-10b is 'open'; the spec is 'draft'.
notes: Deliberately out of the public repo per the open-core boundary; the gate would live in the hosted build.

name: NATS gossip transport (stubbed)
user_story: As an operator on a NATS-equipped network, I want health beats over NATS so that gossip scales beyond a shared filesystem.
expected_behavior: NatsGossipTransport lazily require('nats') in connect(); publishes/subscribes on ac.hb.<agent>. Currently connect() always reports available=false (TODO(nats)) so the gossip ring falls back to the filesystem transport even when the package is present.
gaps: connect()/publish()/subscribe() wire calls are TODO(nats) placeholders — connect() proves the package resolves then returns false; no real publish/subscribe ever happens
notes: Honest stub: available stays false so the FS ring is always used. The GossipTransport interface is the seam.

name: A2A Agent Card publisher (DESIGN Gap D)
user_story: As an A2A-aware peer, I want a discoverable agent-card.json per registered agent (plus an orchestrator card) so that I can find and talk to the fleet via the well-known path.
expected_behavior: publishAgentCards writes <root>/.well-known/<id>/agent-card.json plus an agent.json A2A-canonical alias for every registry agent and the orchestrator, and returns a registry copy with agent_card_path set per agent. IO-injected writer; idempotent overwrite.
gaps: No production caller found (grep shows references only in extension.ts import region check, onboarding, and tests) — the publisher is exported + tested but not invoked from a command/tick I could locate
notes: agentCardPublisher.test.ts covers it. Builds on agent-card.ts buildAgentCard (A2A v0.2.5).

name: Cloud login / token auth (D.1) — PAT or OAuth device flow
user_story: As a Pro user, I want to authenticate to the relay by pasting a PAT or completing a web OAuth device flow so that forwarding can use a token scoped to my installation.
expected_behavior: cloudLogin stores a token (PAT path is offline; oauthIssuer path drives an RFC-8628 device flow via fetch) scoped to installation_id, in the OS keychain (lazy keytar) or an AES-256-GCM encrypted file fallback (chmod 0600 / icacls on Windows). getCloudToken rejects scope_mismatch/expired/corrupt. cloudLogout revokes+deletes; rotateToken replaces in place.
entry_points: cloudLogin() | cloudLogout() | rotateToken() | getCloudToken() | resolveSecretStore() | resolveInstallationId()
gaps: No command or CLI entry point wires cloudLogin — extension UI says 'Run AutoClaw: Cloud Login' but no autoclaw.cloud.login command exists in package.json; login is reachable only via the exported API. keytar is intentionally not a declared dependency (TODO(keytar-dep)), so the encrypted-file fallback is the default backend.
notes: Tested in cloudRelay.test.ts (auth paths). Strong token hygiene; per-machine scoping enforced on read.

name: Governance approval gate + append-only audit log (AF-5)
user_story: As an org admin, I want an approval gate plus an audit log so that human-in-loop and governance-controlled actions require sign-off and every dispatch is traceable.
expected_behavior: gateDispatch(type, controlLevel) returns needsApproval=true for governance control level or human-in-loop types; appendAuditLog writes JSONL rows to .autoclaw/orchestrator/audit/<date>.jsonl; readAuditLog reads them back tolerant of missing/malformed files.
gaps: Logic-only: gateDispatch / audit log are not invoked by any production dispatch path in extension.ts or the orchestrator loop — no caller wires the gate into actual task dispatch
notes: BACKLOG marks AF-5 'done (logic)'. Tested in fabricRoutingGovernance.test.ts.

name: LMD multi-machine gossip ring (E4) — cross-machine health view
user_story: As an operator watching machine B, I want machine A's stalled agents to be visible so that I get one fleet-wide health picture without LLM routing.
expected_behavior: GossipRing publishes the local health snapshot per agent on a timer to a transport, ingests peer beats into a RemoteHealthTracker (freshest-beat-wins), evicts stale remotes, and emits peer_health / remote_stall (cross-machine stall alert) events. Default transport is the filesystem ring under comms/gossip/.
gaps: GossipRing is defined + tested but NOT instantiated in any production code path (extension.ts / CLI) — grep finds it only within gossip.ts and tests; the multi-machine LMD health ring is dormant
notes: Distinct from the cluster-map gossip (election) and the cloud relay. Tested in lmd-gossip.test.ts.

name: LMD stall recovery — re-kick / dead handling / quorum exclusion
user_story: As an operator, I want stalled agents re-kicked, dead agents toasted and excluded from consensus, and recoveries logged so that the fleet self-heals without LLM cost.
expected_behavior: On health_change: stalled => dispatch a re-kick prompt via the registered runner (logs 'no_runner' when none); dead => OS toast + consensusEngine.excludeAgent; recovered => consensusEngine.restoreAgent. All actions appended as JSONL to .autoclaw/runtime/keepalive.log.
entry_points: new StallRecovery() | StallRecovery.start()/stop() | RunnerLookup / ConsensusEngineBridge seams
gaps: RunnerLookup and ConsensusEngineBridge default to stubs that warn 'wire to real engine in a future sprint' — re-kick dispatch and quorum exclusion are no-ops unless a caller injects real implementations
notes: Tested in lmd.test.ts. The seams are intentional; production wiring (fleet-start) injects what it can.

name: Pluggable cross-agent message bus (FabricBus: fs/ws/nats drivers)
user_story: As an extension developer, I want a pluggable pub/sub bus with fs/ws/nats drivers so that fast-path notifications fan out without replacing the durable filesystem mailbox.
expected_behavior: createFabricBus({driver}) returns an fs no-op bus, a ws bus wrapping BridgeEventBus, or a lazily-loaded NATS bus; the nats driver falls back to fs with a one-line warning when the optional 'nats' package or server is unavailable. Wired in extension.ts via autoclaw.fabric.busDriver / natsUrl settings.
entry_points: createFabricBus() | autoclaw.fabric.busDriver setting | autoclaw.fabric.natsUrl setting | compileTopicMatcher()
gaps: fs driver is a deliberate no-op (publish/subscribe never deliver — FS-only relies on inbox polling) | nats driver is real but its delivery in production depends on the optional 'nats' package being installed; default config driver is 'fs'
notes: fabric.test.ts covers all three drivers + topic matcher. ws driver repurposes BridgeEventBus 'message' channel with an __ac_topic envelope.

name: Agent-type taxonomy (coder/runner/auditor/supervisor/assistant/governance)
user_story: As an orchestrator, I want agents classified by what they DO so that I direct work and route reviews by kind with the right trust, consensus, and human-in-loop posture.
expected_behavior: agentTypeProfile(type) returns per-type defaults (trust preset, consensus rule, humanInLoop, canOrchestrate, capabilityTags). Auditor => unanimous consensus + read-only; assistant/governance => human-in-loop. Persona and runner ids map to a default type.
notes: Pure + vscode-free. Consistent with reviewSla SECURITY_TIER_PERSONAS (auditor => unanimous). Tested in fabric.test.ts.

name: Capability-aware score-based task router (AF-9)
user_story: As an orchestrator, I want to pick which agent should take THIS task now given capability, language, trust, load, cost and phase so that work routes to the best eligible agent.
expected_behavior: routeTask/routeTasks compute score = capability_match × language_match × trust_score × idle_factor × cost_factor × phase_factor; ineligible agents (unavailable, at capacity, untrusted, criticality-1 below medium trust) score 0; no eligible agent => fallback:true with a round-robin note. Multi-task pass increments in-pass load to avoid one agent absorbing all tasks.
entry_points: routeTask() | routeTasks() | scoreAgent() | agentsFromOffers() | fabric.route MCP tool
notes: Surfaced read-only over MCP as fabric.route (reads registry.json + live capability_offer messages). Tested in fabricRouter.test.ts + fabricRouteTool.test.ts.

name: Cloud relay client — inert-by-default heartbeat/inbox forwarding (D.2)
user_story: As a Pro user, I want my local heartbeats and inbox messages forwarded to a hosted relay so that a web dashboard shows a cross-machine fleet view — but only after I explicitly opt in.
expected_behavior: CloudRelay.sendHeartbeats (batched+gzip, 60s cadence) and sendInbox (AES-256-GCM-encrypted payloads) POST to /v1/heartbeat and /v1/inbox; every send is a safe no-op (skipped: relay_disabled/no_token/...) unless enabled+https endpoint+stored token (+GA consent). Failed sends go to a bounded on-disk retry queue; flushQueue retries up to 6× then drops.
notes: Inert by default; HTTPS-or-loopback enforced; bearer token only in Authorization header, never queued/logged. Wired into the 30s relay tick in extension.ts. Tested in cloudRelay.test.ts.

name: Cloud relay consent commands (SEC-2 / RELAY-WIRE)
user_story: As a user, I want an explicit consent modal naming the endpoint and what is forwarded before any cross-machine forwarding so that nothing leaves my machine without informed opt-in.
expected_behavior: autoclaw.cloud.enableRelay prompts for an https endpoint, shows a consent modal, and writes relay-config.json with tier:ga + consentAckAt; autoclaw.cloud.disableRelay sets enabled:false (inert) while keeping settings.
notes: GA tier requires consentAckAt before relayIsActive returns true. Tested in cloudSection.test.ts.

name: Cloud relay cross-machine PULL (AF-7b / AF-10c)
user_story: As a multi-machine user, I want my other machines' messages and heartbeats pulled down so that each machine sees the whole fleet's inbox + health.
expected_behavior: CloudRelay.fetchInbox GETs /v1/inbox?to=…, decrypts payloads locally, skips undecryptable; fetchHeartbeats GETs /v1/heartbeat (clear) and returns rows with installation_id. forwarding.applyFetchedToInboxes writes pulled messages into recipient inboxes idempotently; applyFetchedHeartbeats caches remote rows (dropping own installation) for the fleet view.
notes: Wired into the relay tick (fetchInbox + fetchAndCacheHeartbeats). Tested in cloudRelay.test.ts + cloudForwarding.test.ts.

name: Cross-window cluster-map gossip (E3a) — election convergence accelerator
user_story: As an orchestrator window, I want to learn of a remote leadership takeover promptly so that I stand down without waiting for the slow filesystem poll.
expected_behavior: ClusterMapGossipBus publishes the host's versioned ClusterMap atomically (one file per origin) and readBeats returns fresh peer beats; RemoteClusterMapTracker merges by integer (epoch,term) into an advisory best-seen map that is NEVER written to disk and NEVER used to elect/steal/renew. Wired one-shot per orchestrator tick.
notes: Wired into orchestratorLoop runTick (publish if active + journal a peer-newer signal). Advisory/wake-only by construction; wx-locked cluster-map.json stays sole authority. Tested in clusterMapGossip.test.ts. (LAN relay over this bus lives in src/fleet/lanGossipRelay.ts, out of scope.)

name: Fabric onboarding — register a platform runner as a typed worker (AF-4)
user_story: As an operator, I want to detect, health-check, and register an existing platform runner as a typed fabric worker so that the orchestrator can route work to it by kind.
expected_behavior: onboardPlatform detects a runner, health-checks it, and upserts a RegisteredAgent with its agent_type + can_orchestrate; a not-detected platform is reported but not registered; detected-but-unhealthy is still registered with the failure noted; idempotent. Exposed via the autoclaw.fabric.onboard command (QuickPick over runners).
notes: Wired as a VS Code command. Tested in fabricOnboarding.test.ts.

name: Live heartbeat + inbox forwarding wiring (RELAY-WIRE / AF-7)
user_story: As a user with the relay enabled, I want forwarding to happen automatically on the heartbeat tick with once-only inbox dedup so that the dashboard stays current without manual action.
expected_behavior: On each 30s tick (guarded against overlap), the extension calls forwardHeartbeats, forwardInbox (per-message forwarded_at marker so each message relays once; marked only when actually sent/queued), flushQueue, fetchInbox, and fetchAndCacheHeartbeats — all safe no-ops when the relay is inert.
entry_points: forwardHeartbeats() | forwardInbox() | gatherHeartbeatsForRelay() | gatherInboxForRelay() | extension.ts relay tick
gaps: BACKLOG lists 'inbox forwarding at the inbox-write site' as still open — current forwarding is tick-driven (a poll), not write-site-hooked, but it is wired and dedups correctly
notes: session_id never carried (SEC-1). Tested in cloudForwarding.test.ts.

name: LMD health state machine (alive/degraded/stalled/dead)
user_story: As an operator, I want a deterministic health state machine driven by missed-beat counts so that an agent's degradation/recovery is classified consistently.
expected_behavior: tick() advances per-agent state by consecutive missed beats: 2=>degraded, 5=>stalled, 10=>dead; a newer heartbeat timestamp resets to alive. Fires stateChange events with from/to/at. Tracks rekickSent for escalation.
notes: Pure in-memory, no IO. Tested in lmd.test.ts.

name: LMD heartbeat reader — zero-token health polling
user_story: As an operator, I want agent heartbeat files polled every 30s with no LLM/network cost so that I get a live health grid for the fleet panel.
expected_behavior: HeartbeatReader reads .autoclaw/orchestrator/comms/heartbeats/<agent>.json on a 30s interval, parses the timestamp, ticks the HealthStateMachine, emits health_change on transitions, and exposes getHealthGrid(). Missing/unparseable file counts as a missed beat.
notes: Wired into the CLI fleet-start / fleet-watch runners. Tested in lmd.test.ts. Pure file IO.

name: Relay payload encryption (AES-256-GCM) + heartbeat minimization
user_story: As a privacy-conscious user, I want inbox payloads encrypted at rest server-side and heartbeats stripped of internal ids so that the relay only ever holds ciphertext for sensitive bodies.
expected_behavior: encryptPayload/decryptPayload wrap inbox bodies in an AES-256-GCM envelope keyed by scrypt(token, installation_id); the relay stores ciphertext only. RelayHeartbeat deliberately omits session_id (SEC-1 minimization); heartbeats are forwarded in clear with a per-channel opt-out.
entry_points: encryptPayload() | decryptPayload() | EncryptedEnvelope | RelayConfig.forward channel switches
notes: Key derived from the bearer token (couples to token rotation — noted as MVP). Tested in cloudRelay.test.ts.

name: Relay server bearer-token → account auth (AF-10)
user_story: As a self-hoster, I want a token to map to an account (the set of my machines) so that only my machines exchange messages on a shared namespace.
expected_behavior: resolveAccount parses Authorization: Bearer <token> and looks it up in a token→account map; unknown/absent => null => 401. loadConfig merges env (AUTOCLAW_RELAY_TOKENS=tok:acct,...) over an optional JSON file, env wins.
notes: This is the documented seam where the HOSTED variant would add a subscription/entitlement check. Tested in relayServer.test.ts.

name: Route work + reviews by agent TYPE (AF-3)
user_story: As an orchestrator, I want to rank agents and select reviewers by agent kind (e.g. auditors) so that reviews are assigned to the right type with the right consensus rule.
expected_behavior: rankAgentsForCapabilities ranks by Jaccard overlap of (declared caps ∪ type tags); an optional requiredType filters to that kind; selectReviewers returns agents of a kind; reviewConsensusRuleFor returns the type's consensus rule (auditor => unanimous).
notes: Layered under the AF-9 score router (kept for type-only routing). Tested in fabricRoutingGovernance.test.ts.

name: Self-hostable relay server (AF-10) — store-and-forward
user_story: As a self-hoster, I want a small dependency-free relay server so that my machines coordinate cross-machine for free without a hosted service.
expected_behavior: createRelayServer serves POST /v1/heartbeat, POST /v1/inbox, GET /v1/inbox?to= (drain), GET /v1/heartbeat (fleet view), and unauthenticated GET /v1/health. RelayStore persists per-account messages (drain = at-most-once delete-on-read) and latest-per-agent heartbeats, never decrypting inbox bodies. Run via npm run relay:serve; refuses to start with no tokens configured.
entry_points: createRelayServer() | npm run relay:serve | AUTOCLAW_RELAY_TOKENS / _DATA_DIR / _PORT env | RelayStore
notes: Matches the cloud client contract. Path segments sanitized against traversal. Tested in relayServer.test.ts.

name: Intelligence refresh/index-watch services + (hidden) watch commands
user_story: As a user, I want to start/stop the per-host context refresh service and an index watch so that intelligence stays fresh as I edit.
expected_behavior: autoclaw.intelligence.startRefreshService/stopRefreshService manage a RefreshServiceHandle; autoclaw.intelligence.startWatch/stopWatch manage an IndexWatchHandle. deactivate() stops both. The refresh-service commands are in package.json; the startWatch/stopWatch commands are registered in code but NOT contributed in package.json.
gaps: autoclaw.intelligence.startWatch / stopWatch are registered in extension.ts but absent from package.json contributes.commands, so they are not discoverable in the command palette (callable only programmatically).
notes: grep confirmed 0 occurrences of startWatch/stopWatch in package.json.
