user_story: As a joining agent, I want to score myself against unmet needs and self-elect the best-fit role so that a crowd of arrivals self-distributes instead of dogpiling one lane.
expected_behavior: electRole picks the highest positive score (capability_match × idle × trust / cost) over needs; distributeRoles greedily assigns multiple agents one role each, no need twice; shouldReRole proposes a different role only when the current lane drains.
gaps: Pure scoring/assignment functions only — no module in src/fleet auto-applies an electRole result (no claim/lease write here); the consume side is the caller's job
notes: Deterministic, no clock/randomness. Tested in roleElection.test.ts.

name: Workforce signal ingest (resume building from comms)
user_story: As the talent pool, I want real fleet signals folded into resumes so that reputation reflects what actually happened, not self-assertion.
expected_behavior: Scans the shared comms inbox for task_complete (success) and scope_violation, maps each to an Outcome, folds it into the right worker's resume; idempotent via a per-workspace watermark of processed message ids; runs each orchestrator loop tick.
gaps: Consensus review outcomes (review_passed/failed) deliberately deferred — need reliable task->author attribution
notes: Wired into orchestratorLoop. Tested in workforceIngest.test.ts.

name: Workforce talent pool + earned resume
user_story: As the org layer, I want a standing per-worker record with a resume built from real signals so that I can recall a proven worker and weight routing by reputation.
expected_behavior: Worker records (roles_can_play, skills, llms, tools, status, trust, earned Resume) persist under ~/.autoclaw/workforce/<id>.json; applyOutcome/foldOutcomes fold task_complete/consensus/scope_violation/cost into the resume; malformed-tolerant.
gaps: No user-facing command surfaces the workforce pool directly; consumed only by recall/performance/evict + workforceIngest in the orchestrator loop
notes: Pure core + fs persistence. Tested in workforce.test.ts.

name: Cross-tool presence beacons
user_story: As an agent working from another IDE or a headless runner, I want to drop a JSON beacon file so that the AutoClaw fleet panel sees me even without the VS Code extension.
expected_behavior: Any agent writes one beacon JSON (machine-global ~/.autoclaw/beacons or workspace comms/beacons); readers normalize it (origin, workspace_id, staleness vs 5-min TTL), dedup by (agent_id, session_id) keeping the freshest, and skip malformed/stale files silently.
notes: Pure of vscode; BEACON_TTL_MS=5min. Tested in fleet-beacons.test.ts.

name: Discovered-but-untrusted trust ceiling (single source of truth)
user_story: As a security-conscious operator, I want LAN-discovered peers treated as observe-only telemetry so that an unauthenticated peer can never silently slip into a trust/admit/census sink.
expected_behavior: isDiscoveredUntrusted(b) returns true for origin 'lan'; every trust sink (pending tray, needs census, panel merge) calls it, so a discovered peer is excluded from admit, role-coverage, and dispatch until T2 auth.
gaps: T2 (mTLS/SVID/biscuit) authentication that would promote a 'lan' peer to trusted is not yet built — discovery stays observe-only by design
notes: Centralized guard so a new untrusted origin is added in one place.

name: FLEET-DIGEST (single fleet-status.json per SYNC)
user_story: As a looping agent, I want one canonical fleet-status.json so that each SYNC I read one file instead of re-walking beacons + every inbox + claims + board.json.
expected_behavior: buildFleetDigest takes the panel's FleetDashboardModel (+ optional board) and a timestamp argument and returns a deterministic digest; serializeFleetDigest writes it to .autoclaw/orchestrator/comms/fleet-status.json (writer in extension host).
notes: Pure (no Date.now); derived from the same render model. Tested in fleetDigest.test.ts.

name: Join invite tokens (single-use, scoped, TTL'd)
user_story: As a maintainer, I want to issue a single-use scoped token so that an outside agent (Codex, Hermes, another IDE) can join my project bounded by construction.
expected_behavior: createInvite mints a random token with path scope, trust ceiling (default 'off'), admit_policy (default 'auto-preapproved'), 24h TTL; consumeInvite is single-use (fails not_found/expired/already_consumed); revokeInvite deletes it; deny-by-default on malformed.
notes: Command wired at extension.ts:858. admitDecision is pure policy resolution. Tested in invites.test.ts.

name: Join-prompt generator (one-click ready-to-paste)
user_story: As a maintainer issuing an invite, I want a complete ready-to-paste prompt tailored to the target tool's join lane so that the invited agent isn't left holding a bare token.
expected_behavior: Given a target tool, workspace, role/scope, and invite token, renders one prompt for the tool's lane (mcp / http / fs / slash) with the six-phase loop steps; pure, type-only Invite import keeps it fs-free.
notes: Command wired extension.ts:863. fleet-templates.test.ts covers join targets/lanes.

name: LAN cluster-map gossip relay (T1, advisory wake-only)
user_story: As a multi-host operator, I want this host's cluster-map beats bridged to LAN peers so that windows on other machines wake on each other's coordination changes — without trusting an unauthenticated peer.
expected_behavior: LanGossipRelay broadcasts locally-originated ClusterMapBeats to discovered peers + seeds on a distinct port (48485) and mirrors received beats into the FS gossip bus re-keyed under a one-hop 'lan:' origin and receive-clamped; relayed beats are wake-only (never merged/elected on); origin-rotation flood capped at 256; gated like discovery + its own consent.
entry_points: LanGossipRelay | encodeClusterMapBeat() | parseClusterMapBeat() | remoteBeatToLocal() | autoclaw.cluster.lan.gossip setting
gaps: Advisory-only by design until T2 authenticates peers; relayed beats can at most cause a wasted board-refresh wake
notes: Requires LAN discovery on + distinct port. Tested in lanGossipRelay.test.ts.

name: LAN discovery socket adapter (off-by-default, consent-gated)
user_story: As an operator, I want LAN peer discovery that binds no socket unless I both enable the flag and acknowledge consent so that nothing is shared without an explicit opt-in.
expected_behavior: LanDiscovery.start() is a hard no-op unless shouldStartLanDiscovery (flag + consentAckAt) passes; binds UDP (seed or multicast 239.255.42.99), announces on an interval, writes discovered foreign announces as origin-'lan' workspace beacons; idempotent start/stop; drops own echoes; best-effort (never throws).
notes: Wired via void startLanDiscovery (extension.ts:3623). Default OFF. Tested in lanDiscovery.test.ts + lanDiscovery-socket.test.ts.

name: LAN presence wire protocol (announce build/parse/project)
user_story: As a peer on the same LAN, I want a strict non-sensitive presence announce so that others can discover me without leaking workspace path, task, IP, or tokens.
expected_behavior: buildSelfAnnounce emits {v, machine_id, host, port, ts}; parseAnnounce fail-closes on oversized (>512B)/malformed/wrong-version/bad-charset; announceToBeacon projects to an origin-'lan' beacon stamped with the SOURCE address + RECEIVE clock, deduped by lan:<machine_id>.
notes: Pure socket-free core. Tested via lanDiscovery.test.ts.

name: Newcomer-agent scaffolder
user_story: As a brand-new agent not in the hardcoded 9-extension list, I want my inbox/registry-row/rules file created so that I can actually send messages, claim tasks, and be revived.
expected_behavior: scaffoldAgent idempotently creates inboxes/<id>/{_state,processed}, a registry.json row carrying loop_mechanism + keepalive_template (per-agent KEEPALIVE_PROFILES, plain-message default for unknowns), and a bootstrap rules.md; safe to re-run, won't clobber a customized rules file; guards against path traversal in the id.
notes: Command wired extension.ts:869. Tested in fleet-scaffold.test.ts.

name: Pending-tray admit / decline
user_story: As an operator, I want agents that checked in but aren't in fleet.json shown in a tray so that I can admit (give role+trust) or decline them.
expected_behavior: computePendingAgents returns fresh-beacon agents absent from fleet.json (suggested role/type/trust from a consumed invite, else beacon hints; LAN peers and stale beacons excluded); admitAgent writes the agent into fleet.json (read-modify-write, preserving peers).
notes: Commands wired at extension.ts:859-860. Tested in pending.test.ts.

name: Per-agent fleet metrics rollup (tokens / cost)
user_story: As an operator, I want per-agent token + dollar figures so that the agent card and Manager show real metrics instead of guessing.
expected_behavior: buildAgentMetrics takes parsed LLM cost-ledger rows + an attribution hint map + timestamp and returns per-agent + fleet-total rows (costUsd = costCents/100); rows with no resolvable agent land in an 'unattributed' bucket so totals reconcile; readLlmLedgerRows is the thin fs reader.
gaps: Attribution is best-effort: a single-agent setup with no session id on rows lands everything in 'unattributed' (expected, not a bug)
notes: Reads .autoclaw/llm/cost-ledger.jsonl (one of three ledgers). Command wired extension.ts:790. Tested in fleetMetrics.test.ts.

name: Project needs vector (role-coverage gap, open lanes, staleness)
user_story: As an arriving agent, I want a 'what the project needs now' vector so that I can self-elect the role the project is short on.
expected_behavior: computeNeeds derives role_coverage_gap = (lane roles ∪ declared roles) − roles covered by a fresh live agent, plus open lanes, stale claims, and unclaimed findings, with a human summary; gatherNeedsInput reads fleet.json/board.json/reconcile-report/beacons; writeNeeds/readNeeds persist needs.json (LAN peers excluded from coverage).
gaps: plannedLanes are injected by the caller (it parses sprint YAMLs); needs.ts itself does not parse YAML
notes: Pure compute + thin fs adapter. Tested in needs.test.ts.

name: Role -> agent_type derivation map
user_story: As a join wizard, I want to ask the user only for the richer 13-value role and derive the 6-value behavioral agent_type so that the two facets never collapse or contradict.
expected_behavior: ROLE_TO_AGENT_TYPE maps each canonical role to its default behavioral type (many-to-one); agentTypeForRole / deriveAgentType are total (unknown/empty -> assistant); ROLE_TYPE_ALTERNATES lists declared overrides for ambiguous roles.
notes: Pure; intentionally non-invertible. Tested in roleType.test.ts.

name: Team templates gallery (10 ready-made squads)
user_story: As a new user, I want to start from a named multi-agent team recipe so that I don't have to configure each agent from a blank picker.
expected_behavior: TEAM_TEMPLATES exposes 10 squads (solo+reviewer, feature-build, review-gauntlet, security-audit-cell, etc.), each seat pre-filled with role+agentType+tool+scope+admit policy+rationale; invariants enforce role∈13, type∈6 (default or declared alternate), tool∈JOIN_TARGETS, admit∈policies, unique ids, exactly one recommended.
notes: Data layer only; per-seat invite fan-out in extension.ts:5248. Tested in teamTemplates.test.ts.

name: UI-driven fleet.json / needs.json authoring
user_story: As a user on a fresh project, I want a starter fleet.json scaffolded from the agents I already have so that role election has something to read instead of every agent resolving to generalist.
expected_behavior: Scaffolds a starter manifest merging with any existing one (hand-set roles never clobbered, only missing agents added), upserts a single agent's role/type/reports_to, sets the orchestrator, and (re)generates needs.json.
notes: Reuses pending.ts + needs.ts persistence. Commands wired extension.ts:852-853. Tested in fleet-authoring.test.ts.

name: User-authoritative fleet architecture resolver (roles / types / orchestrator)
user_story: As a user, I want fleet.json to override every auto-detected signal so that I stay in ultimate control of who is the orchestrator and what role each agent plays.
expected_behavior: resolveRole applies precedence fleet.json -> settings -> registry -> activity inference -> generalist, preserving custom labels; resolveType degrades unknown types to coder; resolveOrchestrator picks fleet.json.orchestrator -> governance.primary -> first can_orchestrate, only among present agents.
notes: Pure module. Tested in fleet-architecture.test.ts.

name: Connected-tools detection + recall surfaces (panel)
user_story: As a developer, I want AutoClaw to detect other AI tools present on disk and surface how they recall shared context so that cross-tool ingestion is discoverable.
expected_behavior: detectConnectedTools probes adapter discover() (best-effort, throw=>not present) and decideIngestion picks present-but-not-enabled tools to offer auto-enable; recallSurfaces() lists the live MCP/HTTP/file channels for the panel/docs.
gaps: The vscode-side prompt + config persistence that ACTS on IngestionSuggestion is noted as wired by a later integration step; this module is only the testable pure core.
notes: Tests: intelligence-connectedtools.test.ts. Auto-enable prompt wiring lives in the command/extension layer.

name: Intelligence dashboard webview
user_story: As a developer, I want a panel view of intelligence health, metrics, sources, and storage so that I can manage the layer without the command palette.
expected_behavior: The autoclawIntelligenceDashboard view renders health, metrics/dashboard data, sources, and storage status with action buttons mapping to the intelligence commands.
gaps: No dedicated *.test.ts for the dashboard webview rendering; underlying data providers (health/metrics/storage) are tested.
notes: Webview UI glue; data sources are covered by their own tests.

name: Always-on incremental re-index watch service
user_story: As a developer, I want the code index updated incrementally as I edit so that retrieval stays fresh without manual re-indexing.
expected_behavior: startIndexWatchService debounces file changes (clamped >=MIN debounce), filters via makeShouldIndex, and triggers incremental indexCodebase. Wired into extension activation with a stop handle.
notes: Tests: intelligence-watchservice.test.ts.

name: Build context pack (/context-pack) + MCP intelligence.contextPack + HTTP route
user_story: As an orchestrator, I want a single grounded context bundle for an assigned agent so that any runner (Claude Code, Kiro, Cursor, …) starts grounded regardless of MCP support.
expected_behavior: Combines RAG (code+learnings+style+memory) with durable KG facts into ready-to-read markdown + a structured summary (usedCode, codeHits, learningHits, kgHits, degraded, notes). MCP tool is read-only and writes nothing; caps each max at 20. Degrade-safe; KG falls back to full-text.
entry_points: autoclaw.intelligence.contextPack (command) | intelligence.contextPack (MCP tool) | GET /api/v1/intelligence/context (bridge HTTP) | buildContextPack() (exported API)
notes: Built on generateRAGPrompt + in-process KG. Tests: intelligence-contextpack.test.ts; MCP in mcp.test.ts.

name: Dimension/signature guard + namespaces
user_story: As a developer, I want vectors keyed by model@dimension and project namespace so that switching models triggers a clean rebuild instead of silently mixing vector spaces.
expected_behavior: checkSignature/signatureTag detect a model boundary; requireForceOnMismatch raises SignatureMismatchError; migrateToNewSignature reindexes into a fresh namespace. namespaces.ts resolves project vs global search scope.
notes: Tests: intelligence-dimensionguard.test.ts, intelligence-namespaces.test.ts.

name: Embedding provider resolution + pinning (auto ladder)
user_story: As a developer, I want the embedding provider auto-detected and pinned so that my vector signature stays stable and retrieval geometry doesn't flap.
expected_behavior: Resolves auto via the ladder router→ollama→transformers→none, probing each rung with a real embed to measure true dimension, pins the chosen real provider to embedding-resolved.json (never pins none), re-validates pin liveness and re-probes if dead. applyEmbeddingPin gives reads the indexer's identity with no network probe.
notes: Tests: intelligence-embeddingresolve.test.ts, intelligence-picker.test.ts.

name: Embedding providers (router / ollama / transformers / none)
user_story: As a developer, I want multiple embedding backends with a deterministic offline fallback so that intelligence works whether or not I have a model server.
expected_behavior: getEmbedding routes to router (OpenAI-compat/Zippy Mesh), Ollama, in-process @xenova/transformers, or a deterministic hashed bag-of-words 'none'. Detect helpers (detectRouter/detectOllama/listOllamaModels) give cheap liveness probes; degrade callbacks let callers track fallback to none.
entry_points: getEmbedding() | embedStrict() | getNoneEmbedding() | detectOllama() | detectRouter() | listOllamaModels()
notes: Tests: intelligence-embeddings.test.ts.

name: Generate RAG-augmented prompt (/rag-generate)
user_story: As a developer, I want a ready-to-paste prompt grounded in my real code, patterns, style, and memory so that any agent starts a task already context-loaded.
expected_behavior: Assembles a prompt: retrieved code chunks, matched learnings (semantic, or preferences.json fallback when degraded), agent-style.md, recent MEMORY.md summary, and explicit agent instructions. Redacts the task text. Degraded backend skips code section + notes it; never throws.
notes: All deps injectable for offline tests incl. forced degraded path. Tests: intelligence-ragprompt.test.ts.

name: Git signals + outcome ranking
user_story: As a developer, I want learned patterns weighted by whether code was actually committed so that durable memory prefers real, shipped outcomes.
expected_behavior: enrichSessionsWithGitSignals validates kept code against recent commits (offline-safe passthrough when no repo); deriveOutcome/weightForSignal rank git_commit > applied_edit > user_approval > none, raising confidence for git-validated runs.
notes: Tests: intelligence-gitsignals.test.ts.

name: Index codebase into vector store (/index-code)
user_story: As a developer, I want my codebase chunked and embedded into a local vector index so that semantic code retrieval reflects my actual project.
expected_behavior: Walks workspace honoring ignoredDirs/fileExtensions, chunks line-aware with overlap, redacts each chunk, embeds + stores tagged by project namespace + file metadata. Supports incremental git-diff selection via last-index.json and --force full rebuild; sweeps deleted files; persists index-health.json; surfaces staleIndex on model change or mid-pass degrade. Degraded backend => 0 indexed, never throws.
notes: --force pre-flight refuses rebuild when provider is down (abortedProviderDown) to avoid poisoning store with 'none' vectors. Tests: intelligence-ragcode.test.ts, intelligence-remediation-v2.test.ts.

name: Install embeddings provider (semantic / Ollama / basic)
user_story: As a developer, I want guided install of an embeddings provider so that I move from low-quality 'none' vectors to semantic retrieval.
expected_behavior: Installs/guides setup of @xenova/transformers (offline) or directs to Ollama/router; resolves installed transformers entry; de-spams prior 'transformers failed' noise; reports installed state.
notes: Tests: intelligence-installembeddings.test.ts.

name: Install vector backend (sqlite-vec)
user_story: As a developer, I want a one-click installer for the native vector backend so that I get high-quality retrieval without manual native build steps.
expected_behavior: Installs sqlite-vec into a configurable dir (project-local .autoclaw/native by default, never forced onto C:), conveys target via spawn cwd to survive spaced paths, reports backend status to the panel.
notes: Tests: intelligence-installbackend.test.ts.

name: Intelligence health snapshot + nudges
user_story: As a developer, I want a fast health snapshot (provider, index freshness, stale signal, learn recency) with one-click remediations so that I know when to re-index/learn/fix a provider.
expected_behavior: getIntelligenceHealth reads provider config+pin+liveness, index-health.json (chunk count, model/dim, stale, commit drift), learn recency, enabled sources; emits green/amber/red status + dedupable nudges mapping to commands (setProvider/indexCode/learn/installBackend/detectProvider). Side-effect-free.
notes: Tests: intelligence-health.test.ts. Feeds the dashboard Health card.

name: KG MCP tools (search / traverse / record / relate)
user_story: As any MCP host agent, I want to record and recall shared thoughts and relations so that cross-agent memory is queryable from outside AutoClaw.
expected_behavior: kg.search (vec/fts/multi, filter by project/agent/since/at) and kg.traverse are read-only; kg.record and kg.relate are write-gated and append to the message ledger. All return {degraded} flag so callers can tell recall is off vs genuinely empty.
notes: Tested in mcp.test.ts.

name: Knowledge Graph store + service (in-process SQLite)
user_story: As an agent, I want a durable bi-temporal graph of shared thoughts so that team decisions and coordination outcomes are recallable across sessions and processes.
expected_behavior: In-process KnowledgeGraph over the ABI-proof SqliteDriver (node:sqlite first, better-sqlite3 fallback) with positional params, vec0 + FTS recall, bi-temporal valid_from/valid_to, traversal, injected embed fn (default 'none'). Per-process cached handle keyed by db path; never throws — degrades to an empty/degraded handle when the driver is unavailable.
notes: Distinct from the vector store; holds coordination facts. Tests: intelligence-kg.test.ts.

name: Learn from past AI sessions (/learn)
user_story: As a developer, I want AutoClaw to distill durable patterns from my past AI coding sessions so that future agent runs reuse what worked and avoid what failed.
expected_behavior: Collects + dedups sessions from enabled adapters, git-validates kept code, mines workflows + coordination outcomes, writes timestamped insight-<ts>.md, merges preferences.json (no clobber), regenerates agent-style.md, appends to MEMORY.md, embeds learnings into the vector store (replacing prior learn vectors per project), records run metrics + effectiveness; returns a LearnSummary. Output never empty (sensible defaults).
notes: Holds preferences.json lock only across durable writes; embedding step degrade-safe (best-effort, never throws). Tests: intelligence-learn.test.ts, intelligence-remediation-v2.test.ts.

name: Metrics dashboard + cost-ledger bridge (/metrics)
user_story: As a developer, I want per-run learning metrics with real token usage and cost so that I can see token savings and trends over time.
expected_behavior: recordLearningRun appends run stats (sessions, kept rate, patterns, est tokens, gitEnriched); aggregateRealTokens joins the existing cost ledger for real tokens + cost; getDashboardData/computeSummary/computeTrends build the dashboard view (MAX_RUNS bounded). Best-effort — a metrics failure never breaks /learn.
notes: Tests: intelligence-metrics-store.test.ts.

name: Per-host context digests + refresh service
user_story: As a multi-IDE user, I want an ambient project-context digest written into each tool's rules dir and kept fresh so that Cursor/Kiro/Windsurf/Continue/Cline agents are passively grounded.
expected_behavior: writeHostContextFiles resolves targets (.cursor/rules, .kiro/steering, .windsurf/rules, .continue/prompts, .clinerules, .agent/rules) and writes formatted digests (onlyExisting option). startIntelligenceRefreshService ticks on a clamped interval (>=1min) rebuilding existing digests; started in extension activation and stoppable via command.
notes: Tests: intelligence-hostcontext.test.ts, intelligence-refreshservice.test.ts.

name: Populate KG from coordination outcomes
user_story: As a team, I want consensus verdicts and review findings recorded as durable graph facts so that context packs surface real decisions instead of an empty graph.
expected_behavior: recordCoordinationToKg and recordOrchestrationEventsToKg ingest comms-tree coordination signals + live orchestration events into the KG, deduped by deterministic id, best-effort (never blocks /learn).
notes: Invoked from learn.ts. Tests: intelligence-kgrecord.test.ts.

name: Retrieve code (/retrieve) + MCP intelligence.retrieve
user_story: As an agent or developer, I want to query my indexed codebase semantically so that I get the most relevant code chunks for a natural-language description.
expected_behavior: Embeds the query under the indexer's PINNED provider (never the raw auto seed) and returns {file, content, score} hits scoped to the project namespace. MCP tool caps limit at 50. Returns [] (never throws) when the vector backend is degraded.
notes: applyEmbeddingPin keeps read geometry consistent to avoid the stale-index loop. MCP tested in mcp.test.ts; retrieval in intelligence-ragcode.test.ts.

name: Scaffold agent style (/scaffold)
user_story: As a developer, I want my learned style emitted as a prependable scaffold so that I can inject project conventions into any new agent task.
expected_behavior: Reads agent-style.md (or generates a default body when absent), optionally focuses on an area, and emits AutoClaw Agent Scaffold markdown.
notes: Covered within intelligence-ragprompt.test.ts.

name: Secret redaction before persist/embed
user_story: As a security-conscious user, I want secrets stripped before anything is indexed, embedded, or written so that my keys and PII never enter the intelligence stores.
expected_behavior: redactSecrets removes targeted secret patterns from all persisted/embedded text; code path uses skipGenericToken so base64/hashes/URLs in code aren't blanket-redacted (preserving retrieval quality).
notes: Applied in learn, ragCode, ragPrompt. Tests: intelligence-redact.test.ts.

name: Semantic knowledge search (/search)
user_story: As a developer, I want to search all stored intelligence (learnings + code) semantically so that I can recall prior knowledge across sources.
expected_behavior: Embeds a query and runs semanticVectorSearch across the project-scoped vector store, presenting ranked hits in the command UI.
gaps: No dedicated *.test.ts directly exercising the search COMMAND path (underlying semanticVectorSearch is covered by vector tests).
notes: Command-layer glue; relies on vector driver covered by intelligence-vector*.test.ts.

name: Session source adapters (Tier 1-3 ingestion)
user_story: As a developer, I want AutoClaw to ingest sessions from many AI tools so that learning reflects all my coding history, not just one tool.
expected_behavior: Registry probes/collects from AutoClaw-native (default ON) plus Cursor, Claude Code, Claude Desktop, Kiro, Gemini, Cline/Roo, Continue, KiloCode, and a generic adapter (third-party default OFF). Per-adapter error isolation, project tagging, cross-source dedup that merges duplicates and raises kept-code confidence on agreement.
notes: Tests: intelligence-sources.test.ts, intelligence-newsources.test.ts, intelligence-tier3sources.test.ts, intelligence-sourceProvenance.test.ts.

name: Sources management command (/sources list/enable/disable)
user_story: As a developer, I want to list and toggle which session sources are learned from so that I control what feeds the intelligence layer.
expected_behavior: listSources renders a report of all sources with enabled/pending-consent state; setSourceEnabled persists toggles; pendingConsentSources surfaces sources awaiting first-run consent.
notes: Covered by intelligence-sources.test.ts.

name: Steering + skill/tool scaffold generation
user_story: As a developer, I want generated steering files and skill scaffolds seeded with my learned conventions so that new agents and tools inherit how this project works.
expected_behavior: buildSteeringMarkdown turns distilled patterns (+ optional system learnings) into a steering file; buildSkillScaffold emits a skill stub seeded with learned follow/avoid conventions. generateSteering command writes the file.
notes: Tests: intelligence-steering.test.ts, intelligence-toolscaffold.test.ts.

name: Storage tiers + relocation (local + system) and Status/Diagnostics
user_story: As a developer, I want to see where intelligence data lives, its sizes/stats, move the backend, and never have data forced onto C: so that I control storage placement.
expected_behavior: gatherStorageStatus reports locations/sizes; resolveBackendDir defaults to project-local; relocateStore moves an installed backend; systemStore holds a cross-project SYSTEM tier (user-chosen dir, projects.json registry, promote/search system learnings). Status/Diagnostics/pickSystemDir/relocateBackend/systemTier commands expose this.
entry_points: autoclaw.intelligence.status / diagnostics / pickSystemDir / relocateBackend / systemTier (commands) | gatherStorageStatus() | ensureSystemStore() | relocateStore()
notes: Tests: intelligence-storage.test.ts, intelligence-systemstore.test.ts.

name: Tool discovery + consent + watermarks
user_story: As a privacy-conscious user, I want installed AI tools auto-discovered with first-run consent and incremental watermarks so that ingestion is opt-in and doesn't re-read everything each run.
expected_behavior: discoverInstalledTools maps installed runners to adapters; ensureFirstRunConsent/recordConsent gate enabling third-party sources; watermark store tracks last-read positions for incremental collection.
notes: Tests: intelligence-discovery.test.ts, intelligence-connectedtools.test.ts.

name: Tool x project effectiveness matrix (/effectiveness)
user_story: As a router/operator, I want ship-rate and token-ROI per tool and per (tool x project) so that model/agent routing can prefer what works best for a given repo.
expected_behavior: computeEffectiveness pivots the session corpus into cells (sessions, shipped, discarded, kept density, est tokens/kept). Snapshot persisted via recordEffectiveness during /learn; the command displays the matrix.
notes: Tests: intelligence-effectiveness.test.ts.

name: Vector backend abstraction (sqlite-vec + Postgres/pgvector)
user_story: As an operator, I want a swappable vector backend so that I can scale from local sqlite to shared Postgres without changing callers.
expected_behavior: getBackendInitializer selects sqlite-vec (default) or postgres from config.backend; an unknown/misconfigured backend degrades to sqlite-vec with a warning. sqlite-vec itself degrades to a no-op handle when the native stack is absent. Postgres backend connects via configured connectionString.
notes: Tests: intelligence-vector.test.ts, intelligence-vector-driver.test.ts, intelligence-postgres.test.ts.

name: Workflow-sequence mining
user_story: As a developer, I want the ordered tool-action sequences that lead to shipped vs discarded work mined so that successful workflows and anti-workflows feed back into guidance.
expected_behavior: Recovers tool-action steps from [tool_use NAME] markers in transcripts, derives per-session outcome, and ranks successful workflows + anti-patterns folded into durable memory (capped). Prose-only sessions degrade cleanly.
notes: Tests: intelligence-workflows.test.ts.

name: MCP extended endpoints + EXTENDED_TOOLS (install-extended.ts: fleet.dispatch, voidspec.sync)
user_story: As an operator, I want extended MCP config (remote endpoints) and dispatch/voidspec MCP tools so that the fleet can reach out-of-tree services.
expected_behavior: Defines ExtendedConfig + installExtended + EXTENDED_TOOLS (fleetDispatchTool, voidspecSyncTool) and endpoint helpers.
gaps: Entire module is orphaned: EXTENDED_TOOLS is never added to READ_ONLY_TOOLS/WRITE_TOOLS or the server tool set; installExtended/EXTENDED_TOOLS imported nowhere outside the file; no test file exists. | fleet.dispatch / voidspec.sync are not reachable by any host.
notes: Dead/unwired code despite being a full implementation. Not in the active server's activeTools().

name: acp/1 connector discovery (manifest scan, code NOT loaded)
user_story: As the host, I want to discover installed connectors and whether each would load WITHOUT executing their code so that doctor/panel can report them safely.
expected_behavior: Scans <ws>/.autoclaw/connectors/<id>/connector.json then ~/.autoclaw/connectors/<id>/connector.json, validates each fail-closed, de-dupes by id with workspace-wins precedence; never imports/executes connector modules; never throws on missing roots. Path traversal confined (basename check).
gaps: discoverConnectorManifests is only consumed by its own test — it is NOT wired into autoclaw doctor or the fleet panel despite the docstring claiming it answers 'what connectors are installed' for them. | npm-scope discovery (@autoclaw/connector-*) is a noted later increment, absent. | No connector LOADER/factory execution exists yet — ConnectorFactory/Connector are types only; runner/source/presence faces are never resolved or run.
notes: Read-only half of Phase 3. The dangerous code-loading half (spawn/edit, worktree-jail, signing/conformance) is unbuilt.

name: autoclaw mcp install — cross-host MCP registry writer (BP2)
user_story: As a developer, I want one command to wire the autoclaw-mcp server into every AI host on my machine so that any agent can read AutoClaw state without me hand-editing each registry.
expected_behavior: Per-host detect (CLI on PATH or config dir exists) -> read -> merge mcpServers.autoclaw -> write, for claude-code/cursor/antigravity/kiro/gemini-cli/continue/cline/windsurf. Idempotent (unchanged/added/updated), round-trip safe. Kiro registered via kiro-cli mcp add. Scope (workspace|global) carried into spawned server env (AUTOCLAW_MCP_SCOPE).
gaps: No VS Code command or CLI bin invokes installAll() in shipped code — only called from installExtended() (itself unwired) and tests; there is no `autoclaw mcp install` entry point actually reachable by an end user.
notes: Logic + idempotency well-tested (mcp.test.ts:738+). The header/docs describe a CLI command that has no bin/command registration.

name: Generic chat injector (producer side, per loop_mechanism)
user_story: As the router/stall-recovery, I want one call to deliver a prompt to any heterogeneous worker by its loop_mechanism so that I wake cli-headless / plain-message / slash-loop agents uniformly.
expected_behavior: selectInjector returns OutboxChatInjector (cli-headless: writes outbox msg + ready flag — the exact contract KiloCodeBridge/headless runners consume) or HostChatInjector (plain-message/slash-loop: posts to host chat, else returns manual-paste prompt; slash-loop wraps as /loop). IO injected for testability.
gaps: selectInjector/OutboxChatInjector/HostChatInjector are exported but consumed nowhere outside their own file + test — the docstring claims the router and stall-recovery use selectInjector, but no orchestrator/router module imports it. It is wired only in scaffold.ts documentation references.
notes: chatInjector.test.ts (8 cases) covers the units; the integration into the live wake/route path is not present.

name: Kilo Code chat bridge (companion-side relay)
user_story: As an orchestrator, I want to relay messages into Kilo Code's chat panel (Kilo has no headless CLI) so that Kilo participates like any other agent.
expected_behavior: KiloCodeBridge watches agents/kilocode/ready (fs.watch shim), drains outboxes/kilocode/<id>.json, posts via vscode.commands.executeCommand trying kilo-code.sendMessage/kilocode.sendMessage/kilo-code.newTask, writes processed/<id>.json audit, clears the ready flag; falls back to an OS toast / stderr when relay fails. Re-entrancy-guarded drain.
gaps: No dedicated test file (no kilocode.test.ts) — only the producer side (chatInjector) is tested. | KiloCodeBridge is not instantiated anywhere in the running extension — by design it ships in a SEPARATE companion extension's activate(), which is not present in this repo, so the relay path is not exercised end-to-end here. | TODO(chokidar) + TODO(vscode-typings) in code: fs.watch semantics vary by OS; lazy eval('require')('vscode').
notes: Implements docs/rfc/runner-bridge-contract.md §6. Logic is complete and self-contained but unwired in this repo.

name: Knowledge-Graph & Intelligence read tools (kg.search, kg.traverse, intelligence.retrieve, intelligence.contextPack, recall.query, fabric.route)
user_story: As any MCP host, I want to recall shared agent thoughts, RAG over the indexed codebase, build a grounded context pack, query memory, and get a routing decision so that I get AutoClaw's intelligence layer over the wire.
expected_behavior: kg.search/kg.traverse query the in-process KG (degrade flag when no SQLite); intelligence.retrieve does RAG (capped 50, empty when unindexed); intelligence.contextPack returns markdown+summary (degrade-safe); recall.query does token-overlap over dream/MEMORY.md (archive tier = not_implemented); fabric.route scores registry agents + capability_offer overlay.
entry_points: MCP tool: kg.search | MCP tool: kg.traverse | MCP tool: intelligence.retrieve | MCP tool: intelligence.contextPack | MCP tool: recall.query | MCP tool: fabric.route
gaps: recall.query archive tier returns not_implemented (depends on KG daemon C.4) | recall.query is a naive case-insensitive token-overlap scan, not embeddings (TODO BP2+ in code) | todo.list returns not_implemented until a spider TODO index exists
notes: mcp-kg.test.ts covers kg.* readers. todo.list (tools.ts:576) is a documented stub awaiting the spider.

name: acp/1 connector manifest contract + fail-closed validation
user_story: As an external tool author, I want one signed connector.json declaring an id + up to three faces (runner/source/presence) so that I become a first-class AutoClaw citizen without forking it.
expected_behavior: validateConnectorManifest is deny-by-default: malformed/absent -> disabled; wrong acp or abiRange major -> shelved (not crash); tier defaults to 3, runner forces tier<=2; unsigned -> unverified flag (loadable only as tier-3-unverified); unknown future fields preserved on round-trip. satisfiesAbiRange parses >= <= > < = comparators fail-closed.
gaps: Signature verification + provenance/SBOM checks are explicitly deferred to Phase 5 — an unsigned manifest is only flagged unverified, never cryptographically verified.
notes: connector-manifest.test.ts (26 cases). Phase 0 contract surface; will become @autoclaw/connector-sdk.

name: autoclaw-mcp stdio JSON-RPC 2.0 server
user_story: As an AI agent host (Claude Code, Cursor, Kiro, …), I want a per-host MCP subprocess speaking JSON-RPC over stdio so that I can read AutoClaw fleet/inbox/recall state mid-session without a daemon.
expected_behavior: Hand-rolled newline-delimited JSON-RPC loop handling initialize / notifications/initialized / ping / tools/list / tools/call; cold-starts lazily, recomputes the active tool set per request, records cost-ledger telemetry per call. serverInfo name=autoclaw-mcp v3.0.0.
entry_points: startStdioServer() | dispatch() | buildContext() | resolveAutoclawDir() | activeTools() | JSON-RPC methods: initialize/tools/list/tools/call/ping
notes: TODO(BP2) in header to swap to @modelcontextprotocol/sdk; deliberately does not depend on the official SDK. mcp.test.ts has 44 cases covering dispatch, context, tools/list.

name: Cost ledger (per-call MCP telemetry)
user_story: As a maintainer, I want every MCP tool call recorded with duration/size/ok/host so that the fleet panel can roll up per-host MCP activity and I can reason about token/cost.
expected_behavior: Append-only cost-ledger rows written best-effort after each tools/call (never blocks/fails the call); args hashed via hashArgs; rolled up into fleet.cards mcpActivity.
notes: Telemetry is best-effort per RFC §8.

name: MCP allowWrites flip from the extension (one-click trust)
user_story: As a user joining via the one-click flow, I want the extension to flip allowWrites for me (explicit opt-in) so that an MCP-lane peer can claim/vote without me hand-editing JSON.
expected_behavior: Read-merge-write of .autoclaw/mcp/config.json flipping only allowWrites, preserving the tools policy map and other keys; mirrors the gate's path/BOM/env precedence exactly. isWritesAllowed reports the gate's effective view. Wired to the autoclaw.mcp.allowWrites command.
notes: mcp-allowwrites.test.ts (14 cases). Env override still wins over the file flip by design.

name: MCP type/contract surface (JSON-RPC, MCP scope, tool handler)
user_story: As a contributor, I want a single typed contract for JSON-RPC messages, MCP scope, tool definitions/results, and failure reasons so that read and write tools stay consistent.
expected_behavior: Defines JsonRpcRequest/Response/Error, McpScope, McpToolDefinition, ToolContext, ToolResult, ToolFailureReason, ToolHandler, JSON_RPC_ERRORS, MCP_PROTOCOL_VERSION; re-exported from src/mcp/index.ts.
notes: Underpins all tool implementations; exercised transitively by mcp.test.ts.

name: MCP write tools — coordination (note.add, inbox.send, inbox.archive, claim.task, consensus.vote, dream.run, presence.beacon)
user_story: As an MCP-lane agent, I want to capture notes, send/archive cross-agent messages, atomically claim tasks, cast consensus votes, request a dream cycle, and check in via a beacon so that I am a first-class participant in the file-bus protocol.
expected_behavior: claim.task uses create-exclusive wx write (EEXIST -> conflict, surfaces owner); inbox.send is idempotent via client_id+ledger; inbox.archive moves to processed/ + stamps archived_at; consensus.vote writes consensus/active/<task>-<agent>.json; dream.run drops a request marker (no LLM in-process); presence.beacon writes a beacon (host stamps host/session, cannot be spoofed). All ledger-audited.
entry_points: MCP tools: note.add, inbox.send, inbox.archive, claim.task, consensus.vote, dream.run, presence.beacon
notes: Mirrors docs/AGENT_SESSION_PROTOCOL §2-4. presence.beacon closes the A2A gap where MCP agents could message+claim but not be visible. RAW_WRITE_TOOLS exported for tool-body unit tests.

name: MCP write tools — Knowledge Graph (kg.record, kg.relate)
user_story: As an agent, I want to record thoughts and directed relations into the shared KG so that other agents can recall them via kg.search/kg.traverse.
expected_behavior: kg.record records a thought (project defaults to workspace basename, agent to session/'mcp'); kg.relate records a directed edge. Both delegate to the in-process KG; degraded (no SQLite) -> ok with degraded:true (no-op persistence). Ledger-audited.
notes: KGC-4. Primary agent write surface for shared thoughts; read side in tools.ts.

name: MCP write tools — LLM registry (llm.chat, llm.models, llm.health)
user_story: As an MCP host, I want to run a chat completion and probe provider models/health through AutoClaw's LLM registry so that I reuse ZMLR-first/oracle-fallback routing instead of building a parallel router.
expected_behavior: llm.chat delegates to LlmRegistry.chat (ZMLR-first, oracle fallback), records ZICO cost + message ledger; llm.models lists per-provider models; llm.health returns reachable/auth/model-count. Gated like writes (probe external providers). Registry factory injectable for tests.
notes: PA-5. Delegates to src/llm registry; no router lives in the MCP slice.

name: OpenClaw HTTP bridge for remote agents
user_story: As a remote/non-IDE agent, I want an authenticated HTTP API (messages, heartbeat, status, claims, consensus, KG, intelligence context, SSE stream) so that I can join the AutoClaw fleet over the network.
expected_behavior: Node http server exposing /health, /api/v1/health, /api/v1/messages (GET/POST + SSE stream), /api/v1/heartbeat, /api/v1/status, /api/v1/intelligence/context, /api/v1/consensus/vote, /api/v1/claims/<id>, /api/v1/kg/* (health/thoughts/relations/search/traverse/export), /api/v1/capsules, evidence endpoints. Bearer token auth with revocation; biscuit + SVID verification; in-process BridgeEventBus pub/sub for SSE/WS push.
notes: Lives at src/bridge.ts (singular), OUTSIDE the assigned src/bridge/ directory, but is the 'OpenClaw HTTP bridge' named in the area hints. bridge.test.ts (65 cases) + bridge-kg.test.ts cover endpoints. Off by default (autoclaw.bridge.enabled).

name: Per-tool authorization policy + write-tool audit trail
user_story: As an operator, I want to withhold a specific write tool while leaving writes broadly enabled and see who tried which write tool so that I have fine-grained control and an audit trail.
expected_behavior: authorizeWriteTool combines scope+allowWrites+per-tool {allow:false,reason} from config.json tools map (deny-by-default never widens). Every write attempt (allowed or denied) appends a capped-500 row to state.json write_tool_audit; successful message-shaped writes also index into message_ledger.
notes: mcpScoping.test.ts (17 cases) covers the policy parser + decision matrix.

name: Read-only fleet/inbox MCP tools (fleet.status, fleet.cards, inbox.read, doctor.run)
user_story: As any MCP-speaking agent, I want to read live fleet status, per-agent cards, my inbox, and a workspace health check so that I can self-orient without the human or the panel.
expected_behavior: fleet.status derives idle/working/stalled/dead per heartbeat; fleet.cards unions heartbeats+state.json; inbox.read lists messages with read/replied/archived flags (defaults to caller session agent, supports 'shared'); doctor.run reports .autoclaw dir/state/comms/heartbeat checks. All pure file readers, concurrency-safe.
entry_points: MCP tool: fleet.status | MCP tool: fleet.cards | MCP tool: inbox.read | MCP tool: doctor.run
gaps: fleet.status scope='program' returns not_implemented (pending C.14 program registry)
notes: Stall thresholds (60/150/300s) duplicated locally to keep the server self-contained.

name: Situational-awareness read tools (presence.fleet, fleet.brief, fleet.digest)
user_story: As a joining or looping agent, I want a single read that folds the whole comms tree (live sessions, claimable tasks, file-scope overlaps, beacon fleet, awaiting-me) so that I can route myself in one call instead of walking every inbox.
expected_behavior: presence.fleet merges machine-global+workspace beacons (freshest-wins, stale dropped); fleet.brief composes buildFleetBrief over workspaceRoot; fleet.digest reads canonical fleet-status.json or rebuilds the same digest on demand (read-only) via gatherFleetData + board.json.
entry_points: MCP tool: presence.fleet | MCP tool: fleet.brief | MCP tool: fleet.digest
notes: presence.fleet is the read counterpart to the presence.beacon write tool; presence-tools.test.ts covers presence pair.

name: Write-gate authorization (scope + allowWrites coarse gate)
user_story: As an operator, I want MCP write tools off unless I explicitly opt in per-workspace so that a mounted MCP peer cannot mutate my project by default.
expected_behavior: checkWriteGate denies unless scope==='workspace' AND allowWrites resolves true; allowWrites read from AUTOCLAW_MCP_ALLOW_WRITES env (true/1/yes) else .autoclaw/mcp/config.json {allowWrites:true}; deny-by-default on any read/parse failure. Active tool set recomputed per request so a toggle takes effect without restart.
entry_points: checkWriteGate() | readAllowWrites() | activeTools() | env AUTOCLAW_MCP_SCOPE / AUTOCLAW_MCP_ALLOW_WRITES
notes: mcp.test.ts asserts write tools absent from tools/list when gate shut.

name: externalRouterUrl peer server
user_story: As a ZMLR user, I want AutoClaw to act as ZMLR's external router peer so that it can reorder ZMLR's candidate list using local state.
expected_behavior: PeerServer is a loopback-only (127.0.0.1) stdlib HTTP server answering POST /llm/peer/route with {suggestedModelIds:[...]}; hard 200ms budget, 10KB body cap, dedupes+caps at 20 ids, never throws (all failures → empty suggestions so ZMLR uses default order).
gaps: Unwired: never instantiated in extension/activation code | The documented opt-in setting `autoclaw.llm.peerEnabled` does NOT exist in package.json contributes.configuration — no real gate | No suggest() implementation ships in production (only test callbacks)
notes: Built + tested (llm-peer-server.test.ts in CI) per S3 spec but not connected to the running extension.

name: Failsafe installer (qwen3:0.6b @ :11435)
user_story: As a user with Ollama, I want a tiny always-on failsafe model so that the oracle ladder always has a bottom rung.
expected_behavior: installFailsafe() is idempotent + memoized: probes :11435; if main :11434 down → {installed:false, reason:'ollama-missing'}; pulls the model via `ollama pull`; if the dedicated :11435 instance is not running it returns pull-failed with instructions to start `OLLAMA_HOST=...:11435 ollama serve` (it does NOT start the instance).
gaps: Its doc claims 'called from registry.ts on first getPreferred()' but registry never imports/calls it — unwired beyond exports | Never spawns the :11435 serve process (S1 ships pull+detect only)
notes: llm-failsafe-install.test.ts in CI; exported but no production caller.

name: Fallback Oracle — endpoint probe, capability heuristics, task scoring
user_story: As the registry, I want a client-side oracle that discovers reachable endpoints and scores models per task so that I can pick a good local model when ZMLR cannot route.
expected_behavior: refresh() probes zmlr/ollama/lmstudio/failsafe endpoints (DEFAULT_ENDPOINTS) and builds a model catalogue; detectCapabilities() regex-classifies tools/thinking/vision/free + estimates size; pick(task,hints) scores per OracleTask (agent/tool/thinking/fast/vision/free), honors requireLocality and rate-limits, returns recommended + up to 5 alternatives.
gaps: Benchmark blending (getBenchScore) deferred — heuristic-only scoring (noted as S4) | No HTTP daemon/matrix-display modes ported from the Bun original (intentional)
notes: llm-oracle.test.ts in CI; verbatim port of an upstream model-oracle.mjs.

name: LLM CostLedger (ZICO-aligned JSONL ledger)
user_story: As an operator, I want per-call token/cost rows so that I can see LLM spend per provider without ever logging prompt content.
expected_behavior: append() writes one sanitized row (provider/model/operation/tokens/costCents/runId, no prompt text) to .autoclaw/llm/cost-ledger.jsonl; readAll/summarizeByProvider roll up; sanitize() strips anything content-like and clamps numbers.
gaps: No production writer: no `new CostLedger(...).append()` exists in non-test code — readers (budget/ceiling, agentCost, fleetMetrics, intelligence/ledgerBridge) consume a file nothing in the LLM flow writes
notes: Class + readers complete and tested (llm-cost-ledger.test.ts in CI); the write path into registry.chat is missing.

name: LlmRegistry.chat() with 429→rate-limit→re-pick
user_story: As an autonomous loop, I want chat() to auto-record 429s and skip rate-limited models so that I do not re-fire paid rate-limits.
expected_behavior: chat() resolves providerRef or getPreferred, calls provider.chat, and on httpStatus===429 records a 60s rate limit in the oracle (default retry-after) so the next pick avoids that (model,endpoint).
gaps: Does NOT write the LLM CostLedger (no append call in registry.chat); cost-ledger writer is unwired | retry-after is hardcoded 60s because ChatResult carries no retry-after
notes: Cost accounting at this layer is a documented intent that is not realized — see CostLedger feature.

name: LM Studio provider adapter
user_story: As a developer running LM Studio, I want its localhost:1234 server treated as a first-class provider so that the oracle's lmstudio-local endpoint becomes a runnable pick.
expected_behavior: Thin OpenAI-compat subclass at http://127.0.0.1:1234 (env LMSTUDIO_HOST override), auth none, registered by default in the registry.
gaps: No dedicated lmstudio test file (only covered transitively); inherits the base's no-streaming limitation
notes: Added so oracle 'lmstudio' endpointType maps to a real provider (endpointToProviderId).

name: Workflow intent router (recommendModel hook)
user_story: As a workflow, I want capability/locality/budget-aware model selection so that each intent gets a model that satisfies policy with an optional ZippyMesh recommendation.
expected_behavior: routeWorkflowIntent() filters candidates by health/locality/cost/context/capabilities, honors an injected recommendModel callback when its pick is eligible, else ranks by benchmark/reputation/context/latency under a routing profile (cheap/balanced/quality/...).
gaps: Lives in src/workflows (not src/llm); its recommendModel is a generic callback hook NOT wired to ZippyMeshProvider.recommendModel — the ZMLR bridge is unconnected | candidates are supplied by the caller; no integration with the oracle's model catalogue
notes: Tested via fabricRouter.test.ts; a parallel routing brain to LlmRegistry, included because it is the workflow-side model selector.

name: `autoclaw llm install` — provider config writer
user_story: As a user, I want a command that wires my LLM providers so that ZMLR/Ollama get a config entry, MCP route, and routing playbooks without manual editing.
expected_behavior: installLlm() does 3 idempotent steps: upsert .autoclaw/llm/config.yaml; add zmlr to workspace .mcp.json; import shipped playbooks (mateam/kdream/embeddings) into ZMLR via /api/playbooks. Unreachable provider → skipped (ok:true). Surfaced via command palette 'AutoClaw: Install LLM Providers' with a quick-pick.
gaps: Hand-rolled YAML parser is not general — a hand-edited config it can't parse yields an empty providers list, causing a duplicate the user must resolve (documented)
notes: llm-install.test.ts in CI; command wired in extension.ts with output channel.

name: LlmProvider abstraction + ProviderRef parsing
user_story: As an extension developer, I want a narrow LlmProvider contract and a ProviderRef parser so that every LLM backend (Ollama, ZMLR, LM Studio) is interchangeable and addressable as `<provider>:<model>`.
expected_behavior: Types-only module defines LlmProvider (detect/chat/models/health/embed), ChatOptions/ChatResult/ChatHints, capabilities, and `parseProviderRef('ollama:llama3.1:70b')` → `{providerId:'ollama', model:'llama3.1:70b'}` (split on first colon); `normalizeMessages`/`mergeCapabilities` helpers.
entry_points: parseProviderRef() | normalizeMessages() | mergeCapabilities() | LlmProvider interface (src/llm/index.ts exports)
notes: Pure types/helpers; covered by llm-types.test.ts (in CI list).

name: LlmRegistry getPreferred() three-branch routing
user_story: As the persona/MCP layer, I want one entry point that picks a provider so that I get explicit→ZMLR-recommend→oracle fallback transparently.
expected_behavior: getPreferred() branch 1 honors explicitProviderId; branch 2 if ZMLR reachable + not rate-limited calls recommendModel; branch 3 refreshes the oracle and picks, mapping endpointType→providerId, falling to alternatives when no adapter exists; returns null only when even failsafe is gone.
notes: llm-registry.test.ts in CI; consumed by personas/provider-stub and mcp/writeTools.

name: MCP llm.* tools (llm.chat / llm.models / llm.health)
user_story: As an MCP-connected agent, I want llm.chat/llm.models/llm.health so that I can run completions and inspect providers through the AutoClaw MCP server.
expected_behavior: Three gated/audited write-tools delegate to the LLM registry: llm.chat runs registry.chat (ZMLR-first/oracle fallback, optional providerRef) and appends a message-ledger entry; llm.models lists each provider's models(); llm.health reports per-provider health. All require workspace-scoped write access.
entry_points: MCP tool llm.chat | MCP tool llm.models | MCP tool llm.health | _setLlmRegistryFactoryForTests()
gaps: llm.chat audits to the message ledger but does NOT write the ZICO LLM CostLedger despite the docstring implying the registry records cost
notes: llmWriteTools.test.ts in CI; gated by checkWriteGate (workspace scope + allowWrites).

name: modelCatalog — model id → context-window lookup
user_story: As the fleet panel, I want each model's max context window so that I can show a context-fill gauge for any model id.
expected_behavior: contextWindowForModel(id) resolves via explicit size marker ([1m]/(200k)) → exact normalized match → longest family-prefix match → DEFAULT_CONTEXT_WINDOW (8k); normalizeModelId strips vendor prefixes/dates/quant tags; never throws; isKnownModel() reports catalog membership.
gaps: Static map needs manual updates as new models ship
notes: Pure/host-free so usable in webview renderers; llm-model-catalog.test.ts in CI.

name: Ollama provider adapter
user_story: As a developer with Ollama on localhost, I want AutoClaw to use it so that I get free local inference without cloud keys.
expected_behavior: Targets http://127.0.0.1:11434 (env OLLAMA_HOST override); overrides models() to use native /api/tags for parameter_size/family and detect() to use /api/version for a real version string; chat via /v1 OpenAI-compat.
entry_points: OllamaProvider (exported) | registered by default in LlmRegistry
notes: Default registry member; llm-ollama.test.ts in CI.

name: OpenAI-compatible base provider (chat/models/detect/health/embed)
user_story: As an agent, I want one OpenAI-REST adapter so that any /v1-speaking server is callable for chat and embeddings with consistent error classing.
expected_behavior: POSTs /v1/chat/completions and /v1/embeddings; GET /v1/models; classifies 429→rate_limit, 401/403→auth, timeout→timeout; returns ChatResult/EmbeddingsResult with tokens+duration; embed() forces `x-intent: embed`; tracks lastChatAt and recentErrorCounts for health().
gaps: No streaming/SSE support despite capabilities.streaming=true (chat always sends stream:false)
notes: Base class for Ollama/ZMLR/LMStudio; exercised indirectly via llm-ollama/zippymesh/registry tests.

name: Oracle model validation (end-to-end probe)
user_story: As the oracle, I want to validate a model with a tiny completion so that a recently-proven model gets a scoring bonus.
expected_behavior: validate(model,endpoint) sends a 'Reply: OK' completion (Ollama /api/generate or OpenAI /v1/chat/completions), records 429s, and marks lastValidatedAt/lastValidatedOk (a fresh OK within 10min adds +10 to score).
gaps: validate() is not auto-invoked inside getPreferred/pick — caller must opt in to call it
notes: Scoring bonus only materializes if a caller runs validate() beforehand.

name: Oracle rate-limit memory with persistent TTL
user_story: As a long-running agent, I want rate-limits to survive process restarts so that I do not re-hit paid 429s across loop cycles.
expected_behavior: recordRateLimit/isRateLimited maintain a (endpoint:model)→resetsAt map; persisted to .autoclaw/llm/oracle-state.json (version 1) unless ephemeral; expired entries pruned on read; handle429 parses retry-after header.
notes: State file under .autoclaw/llm/; best-effort writes never throw.

name: personas provider-stub registry bridge
user_story: As the persona loader, I want a thin bridge over LlmRegistry so that personas get real LLM completions instead of the old stub.
expected_behavior: Lazily constructs a singleton LlmRegistry per workspace and translates registry.chat's ChatResult down to the personas' simpler provider shape; _setRegistryForTests injects a mock.
gaps: No dedicated provider-stub test in the LLM area (covered indirectly)
notes: Phase B replacement for the old provider-stub; the documented consumer of LlmRegistry.

name: ZippyMesh (ZMLR) router provider + recommend_model
user_story: As an agent, I want to route through the ZippyMesh LLM Router so that it can pick the best backend across LAN and cloud and report cost.
expected_behavior: Targets http://127.0.0.1:20128 with bearer ZIPPYMESH_TOKEN; sends both x-intent and x-zippy-intent (+ x-session-parallel/x-session-id for /mateam fan-out); recommendModel() POSTs {tool:'recommend_model'} to /mcp and returns {model, fallbackChain} or null on any failure (404/502/timeout) so the registry falls through to the oracle.
gaps: recommendModel depends on a ZMLR-side /mcp recommend_model route that may not be deployed (older builds 404 → always null)
notes: llm-zippymesh.test.ts in CI; the null-on-failure path is intentional stopgap per S1 docs.

name: requireHosted gate (hosted features that cost the vendor money)
user_story: As the vendor, I want hosted/cloud features gated by a paid license OR a BYO key so that the trial never runs up our hosted bill.
expected_behavior: Returns true for paid license or BYO key; otherwise shows a non-blocking Get-a-license / Use-my-own-key choice and returns false. Trial does NOT satisfy this gate.
gaps: Exported but has NO live caller anywhere in src/ (only referenced in docs and a comment). No hosted feature currently routes through it.
notes: Reference implementation only; awaits a decision on which hosted feature to gate first (per docs/PICKUP).

name: Upgrade-nag rate limiting + trial-ended notice (NagService)
user_story: As a user, I want upgrade reminders rate-limited so that I am never spammed (global <= every 14 days, per-feature <= weekly).
expected_behavior: Tracks last global/per-feature nag timestamps in globalState; shouldShow* gate prompts; showTrialEndedOnce() shows a single trial-ended notice respecting the global nag window.
gaps: NagService is never instantiated anywhere in src/ (no `new NagService`). showTrialEndedOnce and the rate-limit checks are dead code — the actual milestone prompts use the separate support.ts/schedule.ts path instead.
notes: Built but unwired; the live nag path is the support milestone scheduler, not this service.

name: Advanced Orchestration optimising planner (premium engine + free fallback)
user_story: As an orchestrator user, I want a critical-path, capability/reputation/cost-aware sprint optimiser so that parallel agent work is planned optimally.
expected_behavior: Reads .autoclaw/orchestrator/advanced-input.json (seeds a starter template on first run), merges agents from registry.json when omitted, checks pro.orchestrate.advanced (silent), runs premium engine or free fallback, writes advanced-plan.md and opens it, offers upgrade when not entitled.
gaps: Only the free fallback + the input builder ship publicly; the optimiser itself is in the absent private package. Free fallback returns empty assignments/criticalPath and projectedSprints:0.
notes: buildAdvancedInput and the free fallback shape ARE tested (premium-advanced.test.ts).

name: Agent Scorecards (premium engine + free fallback)
user_story: As a team lead, I want per-agent scorecards (actions, tokens, wall time, token share, last-active) so that I can track agent effectiveness.
expected_behavior: Command checks pro.agentScorecards (silent), runs premium engine when entitled else free fallback markdown, opens it, offers upgrade when not entitled.

notes: Free fallback returns {markdown, createdAt} only (no featureTier).

expected_behavior: check()/require() resolve the effective entitlement and apply decideGate; require() shows one polite Compare Plans/Enter License/Use Free Fallback prompt on denial. But gateEnforcementEnabled() defaults FALSE, so every feature is allowed unless the hidden autoclaw.licensing.enforceGates key is set.
gaps: Enforcement is OFF by design (decision: monetization = commercial-use licensing, not per-feature gating). require()/showUpgradeOrFallback have no unit test. With enforcement off, all gate calls short-circuit to allowed.
notes: Intentional per steering/MEMORY: gates built but dormant until a real purchase path exists. enforceGates is a hidden maintainer flag, not in Settings UI.

expected_behavior: Command checks pro.reports.prEvidence (silent), runs the premium engine when present/licensed, otherwise the free fallback produces a basic markdown report; opens it and offers an upgrade prompt when not entitled. Never blocks.
gaps: Only the free fallback ships in this repo (basic markdown stub). The real evidence engine lives in the absent private package. No test for the prEvidence command/fallback specifically (premium-advanced.test.ts only covers advanced-orchestration).

name: withGate command wrapper for orchestrate/autobuild commands
user_story: As a user, I want premium-tier commands to run normally (with a graceful fallback) so that gating never breaks my workflow.

notes: Distinct from the unrelated withGate in src/mcp/writeTools.ts (consent gate, not licensing).

name: 7-day Pro trial (state logic + service)
user_story: As an evaluator, I want a 7-day Pro trial with no account or card so that I can try paid local features before buying.
expected_behavior: On first meaningful use the trial starts (toast), grants effective Pro tier for 7 days, then auto-marks consumed and reverts to Free. Status/start commands report days remaining. Cannot be restarted after consumption.

name: HEAL phase (signal gathering + bounded recovery execution)

Never kills processes, deletes user data, or merges.

name: Sprint state machine (status transition guard)
user_story: As the orchestrator, I want sprint status transitions validated so that a sprint can only move pending→assigned→in_progress→review→approved→merged.

Manifest gate fields (acceptance commands) are read by a scoped YAML reader so review can gate without a YAML dependency.

Never downgrades, never touches blocked, idempotent; applyBoardAutoTransition does the atomic state.json read-modify-write (temp+rename, Windows EEXIST copy fallback), creating entries for signal-only tasks.

Release-only — never touches live work/git.

expected_behavior: tallyConsensus decides approved/changes_requested/rejected as soon as mathematically certain (2/3 majority for tasks, unanimous for security findings), deduping votes to latest-per-voter and counting only declared reviewers. resolvePendingConsensus writes consensus/resolved/<task>.json atomically and clears active stub + per-agent vote files; idempotent. Supports a revise/converge loop (reviseMaxRounds) that sends dissent back for re-vote.

expected_behavior: reviewSla decides when a review_request re-broadcast is due (SLA window) and builds the message; computes the dynamic consensus quorum from agents with a fresh heartbeat (2/3 majority, unanimous for security); and mints/compares UUID claim tokens with a 10s contention window + deterministic lowest-token-wins tiebreak.

name: Self-healing recovery ladder (supervisor)
user_story: As the fleet, I want bounded reversible recovery actions decided from failure signals so that stalls/dead-dispatches/corruption self-heal without runaway storms.
expected_behavior: resolveRecovery is a pure 7-rung ladder: stale claim→steal_claim (only if owner unhealthy AND expired), dead dispatch→redispatch bounded then escalate (max 2 retries), failed-twice→escalate, lease violation→revoke_trust, corrupt comms→quarantine (never delete), unreachable→mark_stale, drift→surface_finding only. Every action carries a non-empty finding (act-then-report); never targets master. propose mode wraps actions for human approval.

name: Per-agent scope.json model + effective-trust resolution (B5, RFC §3/§4)
user_story: As the orchestrator, I want to read/validate/persist agents/<agent>/scope.json and resolve effective per-dispatch trust so allow/deny lists combine deny-by-default with the preset baseline.

name: Preferred-runner selection (RFC §5.5 preference order)
