notes: DEFAULT_PREFERENCE_ORDER puts reputation ahead of cost/latency (registry.ts:139).

name: Reputation-weighted dispatch (HRW-2 / HR-3)
user_story: As a fleet operator, I want proven workers preferred and scope-violators demoted so routing follows earned reputation.
expected_behavior: When reputationByRunnerId is supplied, getPreferred picks the strictly-highest reputation runner; absent the map it is a no-op (default order unchanged). reputationMapFromWorkers() derives agent_id -> reputationScore from the earned resume; explicit/workspace still outrank reputation; a reputation tie defers to cost.
gaps: No non-test caller yet passes reputationByRunnerId into dispatchViaRegistry — the scorer (performance.ts) and the registry consumer exist and are unit-wired, but the live dispatch path in extension.ts does not populate preference.reputationByRunnerId.
notes: performance.ts header itself notes 'the wiring into the registry is a follow-up; this module is the scorer'. Tested in runner-reputation.test.ts.

name: Runner contract / type surface (RFC §2)
user_story: As an orchestrator developer, I want one stable Runner interface so that every vendor adapter speaks the same dispatch/detect/resume/health/cancel contract.
expected_behavior: index.ts re-exports Runner, DispatchOptions, DispatchResult, Capabilities, ScopeDeclaration, HealthReport, ErrorClass, TrustPreset, SessionSummary, DetectionResult etc. as a single import surface; per-vendor adapters implement Runner and the orchestrator only speaks this contract.
notes: Types-only keystone module; no runtime logic. DispatchTaskType ('code'|'execution'|'review') discriminant present (types.ts:207). Exercised indirectly by every runner test.

name: Runner health reporting (RFC §7 doctor surface)
user_story: As an operator, I want each runner to report auth/version/MCP-count/recent-errors so `doctor` can show fleet health.
expected_behavior: Every Runner implements health() returning HealthReport {ok, authPresent, cliVersion, mcpServersConfigured, lastDispatchAt, recentErrors[]}. Each adapter maintains a rolling error tally by ErrorClass and last-dispatch timestamp; claude-code additionally counts MCP servers from user+workspace settings.json and keeps a bounded 50-entry error ring.
gaps: No code in src/runners reads these reports into a doctor UI; the 'autoclaw doctor' surface that consumes HealthReport lives outside this area (referenced in docs/comments).
notes: Health logic implemented uniformly across all 9 runners; claude-code's is the most complete (MCP count + error ring).

name: RunnerRegistry — register + detection lifecycle
user_story: As the orchestrator, I want to register every vendor runner and probe which are usable so I only dispatch to detected hosts.
expected_behavior: register() adds a runner (re-register replaces + clears detection); detect() runs Runner.detect for all in parallel, marks enabled=found, and treats a thrown detect() as not_installed without propagating. list/listActive/listDisabled/get expose state with remediation hints for disabled runners.
notes: Covered via runner-dispatch-registry.test.ts and runner-reputation.test.ts (both call detect() to flip enabled).

name: Trust-preset translation table (RFC §3)
user_story: As a vendor adapter, I want one off/auto/turbo preset translated to host-specific flags so trust policy is consistent across runners.
expected_behavior: TRUST_PRESET_TABLE maps [runnerId][preset] -> flags for claude-code/cursor/kiro/gemini-cli. translateTrust() returns the row, downgrades to the closest stricter preset a runner supports (recording downgradedFrom + note), and for unknown runners falls back to strictest (off).
notes: Table only covers 4 of 9 runners; CLI runners codex/openclaw/hermes/autogpt define their own local trust maps instead of registering in the table. translateTrust covered via trustPresets.test.ts resolveEffectiveTrust + downgrade test.

name: Architect persona skill
user_story: As a maintainer, I want an architect persona that reads every RFC before drafting new specs so that the design record stays coherent and contradictions are surfaced.
expected_behavior: Invoked via /persona architect (and auto on docs/rfc merges); reads skills/architect/bibliography.md and writes specs to docs/specs/<feature>/. Frontmatter declares trust:auto, preferred_provider ollama:llama3.1:70b with claude-code-runner fallback, consumed by the persona loader.
gaps: Provider dispatch routes through provider-stub.ts (Phase B real src/llm not fully wired through loader); architect not in adapter SKILL_NAMES so no per-IDE adapter is generated
notes: Persona loader (personas-loader.test.ts) tests buildProfile/dispatch/fallback/finding_report generally; architect is one of the personas it loads. Skill is real source but only reachable via the persona surface, not the adapter pipeline.

name: Cross-agent coordination adapter (hand-authored, per host)
user_story: As a foreign IDE agent, I want a cross-agent protocol rule installed in my host's rules dir so that I check the AutoClaw inbox and follow the multi-agent message protocol.
expected_behavior: A cross-agent.md/.mdc/.prompt is shipped to every host (e.g. adapters/claude-code/cross-agent.md) describing the mailbox, message types, send format, claiming, and consensus; renderInboxPrompt() and installAdapters copy/point at it per host.
gaps: cross-agent adapters are NOT generated by the adapter pipeline (no cross-agent in scripts/adapters), so they are hand-maintained per host and can drift from the canonical docs/AGENT_SESSION_PROTOCOL.md
notes: renderInboxPrompt has launcher tests, but the cross-agent.md content itself is hand-authored and not covered by adapters:check. Per-host copies (kiro, cline, cursor, windsurf, antigravity, continue) exist.

name: Doc-Writer persona skill
user_story: As a maintainer, I want a doc-writer persona auto-dispatched when a task touches public API so that docs and CHANGELOG stay in sync and never document unshipped features.
expected_behavior: Invoked via /persona doc-writer and auto on task_complete diffs touching exported types/commands/MCP tools/CLI flags; writes only docs/README/CHANGELOG, never code. Loaded via persona frontmatter (trust:auto, local-first ollama with cloud fallback). Also generates a per-IDE adapter.
gaps: Auto-dispatch on public-API diffs depends on the hook subsystem (triggerHooks) outside this area; provider dispatch still via provider-stub
notes: doc-writer IS in SKILL_NAMES (adapters generated) and in SHIPPED_SKILLS (launcher offers 'Write Docs'). Persona loader provides the dispatch path.

name: Hermes platform-worker skill (assistant runner)
user_story: As a fabric operator, I want personal-assistant work routed to a Hermes REST service with mandatory human-in-the-loop so that scheduling/drafting takes effect only after a person confirms.
expected_behavior: Registers Hermes as agent_type:assistant, trust:off; the runner submits POST /tasks to HERMES_ENDPOINT, polls status, returns a draft for human approval. No direct LLM calls. Frontmatter preferred_provider hermes-runner with no coding fallback.
gaps: No hermes runner test in src/test (no runner-hermes.test.ts); hermes not in adapter SKILL_NAMES so no per-IDE adapter generated
notes: Backing runner src/runners/hermes.ts is substantial (466 lines). Skill is reachable via persona loader + fabric router runner routing, not via the adapter pipeline.

name: Intelligence skill — learning & RAG layer
user_story: As a developer, I want a local-first second brain that learns from past AI sessions and does RAG over my codebase so that context is reused and token waste is cut.
expected_behavior: The intelligence skill surfaces /learn, /index-code, /retrieve, /search, /sources, /rag-generate, /scaffold, /metrics; it explicitly marks /service as Planned and tells the model not to claim a Planned command ran. Backed by real code in src/intelligence/.
gaps: /service watch command documented as Planned/not-yet-available in the skill itself
notes: intelligence is in SKILL_NAMES (gets adapters) and contributes.chatSkills, but NOT in SHIPPED_SKILLS so the Launch Skill picker doesn't offer it. The skill is the user-facing doc over a large real subsystem (src/intelligence/) outside this area.

name: Loop-discipline skill (loop guard rules)
user_story: As an autonomous loop operator, I want six short rules loaded at every loop/watch tick so that the loop reads state from disk, avoids over-supervision, and keeps signals clean.
expected_behavior: Loaded at the start of any perpetual loop, watch-mode tick, or /loop dispatch; six one-sentence rules (read state each cycle, show the loop the world, etc.). Triggered by /loop, /sprint, /work, watch mode.
gaps: Not in adapter SKILL_NAMES (no per-IDE adapter generated), not in SHIPPED_SKILLS (launcher doesn't offer it), no test, no chatSkills entry — discoverable only by a host that already loads it as context
notes: Pure guidance markdown with no codegen or command wiring; relies entirely on the host agent loading the file as ambient context.

name: OpenClaw platform-worker skill (coder runner)
user_story: As a fabric operator, I want scoped coding tasks dispatched to an OpenClaw host (REST or CLI) so that a reviewable result comes back under OpenClaw's own trust levels.
expected_behavior: Registers OpenClaw as agent_type:coder, trust:auto; runner uses OPENCLAW_ENDPOINT REST when set else the openclaw CLI, runs the task under task+scope+verify, and maps OpenClaw job IDs back to AutoClaw task IDs. Fallback provider claude-code-runner.
gaps: No openclaw runner test in src/test (no runner-openclaw.test.ts); openclaw not in adapter SKILL_NAMES
notes: Backing runner src/runners/openclaw.ts is 629 lines (REST+CLI hybrid). Skill reachable via persona/fabric runner routing, not the adapter pipeline.

name: Security-Auditor persona skill
user_story: As a maintainer, I want a security-auditor persona that audits a module against a seeded pattern set and gates security-tier merges so that defects are caught before GA.
expected_behavior: Invoked via /persona security-auditor, by /sprint on auth/crypto/network/secrets tasks, and before any GA flip; writes a structured finding report (severity+disposition) to reviews, reads prior findings from persona memory, and enforces the unanimous-vote security gate.
gaps: The seeded pattern set and merge-gate enforcement are described in the skill prompt; actual gate enforcement lives in src/gateLogic / consensus, not in skills/
notes: In SKILL_NAMES (adapters generated) and SHIPPED_SKILLS (launcher 'Run Audit'). Persona memory accumulation handled by personaMemory subsystem (personaMemory.test.ts).

name: @autoclaw chat participant skill dispatch
user_story: As a Copilot Chat user, I want to invoke AutoClaw skills as @autoclaw /command so that the SKILL.md instructions are injected without copy-pasting prompts.
expected_behavior: The @autoclaw participant maps /kdream, /autobuild, /mateam, /orchestrate, /inbox, /intelligence subcommands to the corresponding SKILL.md, reads the skill body, and streams it (with suggested follow-up buttons) into the chat so the host model executes the skill.
gaps: No test file directly covers chatparticipant skill injection
notes: package.json contributes.chatSkills also registers these 5 SKILL.md paths so the host shows them as native chat skills. /inbox aliases to the orchestrate skill.

name: Adapter drift CI gate (adapters:check)
user_story: As a maintainer, I want CI to fail if committed adapter files drift from the source skills so that hand-edits to generated files are caught.
expected_behavior: `npm run adapters:check` regenerates all adapter files in memory and diffs against disk; exits 0 with 'Adapters in sync' or exits 1 printing a per-file line-level diff summary and instructions to run adapters:build.
gaps: No dedicated unit test for check-adapters; correctness is only exercised when CI runs the script
notes: skills.test.ts asserts the cline/mateam.md adapter carries dispatch strings, which transitively guards drift, but check-adapters.ts itself has no test.

name: Adapter generation pipeline (adapters:build)
user_story: As a maintainer, I want one command to regenerate every per-IDE adapter file from the source skills so that all hosts stay byte-identical to the canonical SKILL.md.
expected_behavior: `npm run adapters:build` loads the 7 SKILL_NAMES skills, transforms each into the per-host format (claude-code SKILL.md, cline .md, cursor .mdc, antigravity .md, windsurf .md, kiro .md, continue .prompt) plus a combined kilocode customModes YAML, and writes only changed files (idempotent).
notes: Per-host transforms: claude-code passes raw verbatim; cursor adds .mdc frontmatter; kilocode emits one combined YAML with verbatim body in a literal block. Tested indirectly via skills.test.ts (regenerated cline/mateam.md carries dispatch language).

name: AutoBuild skill — scheduled workflow engine
user_story: As a developer, I want to schedule and run build/CI workflows from chat so that recurring tasks (build, test, lint-fix) run autonomously.
expected_behavior: The autobuild skill drives schedule/list/run/cancel sub-commands writing .autoclaw/autobuild/workflows/*.yaml, is idempotent on re-schedule by name, uses cross-platform npm-script step commands, and confirms in <=3 lines.
gaps: No test asserting autobuild skill content; no in-repo runtime engine code for workflow execution (skill is prompt-only)
notes: Skill is a markdown contract telling the host model to manage workflow YAML; there is no compiled scheduler in src/ that executes these workflows — execution relies on the host agent following the instructions.

name: Claude Code runner adapter shim
user_story: As the orchestrator, I want a thin adapter that registers the Claude Code headless runner into a RunnerRegistry so that the fabric can dispatch coding tasks to Claude Code.
expected_behavior: adapters/claude-code/runner.ts exports createClaudeCodeRunner/registerClaudeCodeRunner that instantiate ClaudeCodeRunner (optionally with a transport override) and register it; all behavior lives in src/runners/claude-code.ts.
notes: Pure wiring shim; runner-claude-code.test.ts and runner-dispatch-registry.test.ts cover the underlying runner + registry. References docs/rfc/runner-bridge-contract.md.

name: Install adapters for detected IDEs
user_story: As a developer, I want AutoClaw to detect my installed AI extensions and drop the matching adapter rules into their config dirs so that the skills are available without manual copying.
expected_behavior: autoclaw.installAdapters detects Claude Code (global ~/.claude/skills), Cline/.clinerules, KiloCode (.kilocodemodes merge + .clinerules), Cursor/.cursor/rules, Kiro/.kiro/steering, Windsurf/.windsurf/rules, Continue/.continue/prompts, Antigravity/.agent/rules, and ZippyMesh (copies setup guide), then reports what was installed.
gaps: No unit test for installAdapters host-detection/copy logic (heavily vscode/fs-coupled)
notes: Auto-runs on activation when autoclaw.kdream.autoInstallAdapters is set. KiloCode gets BOTH a .kilocodemodes merge and .clinerules copies. ZMLR detection is an HTTP HEAD with 1s timeout.

name: KDream skill — persistent background agent
user_story: As a developer, I want a persistent background agent with auto memory consolidation so that long-running work continues and gets summarized between sessions.
expected_behavior: The kdream skill instructs the host model to manage .autoclaw/kdream/state.json (start/ps/work/add sub-commands), tick the daemon, dispatch work either via the Agent tool or in-session, and report <=3 lines of status. Backed by src/kdream-helpers.ts and the kdream dashboard webview.
notes: Skill is a prompt-contract (markdown), not code; skills.test.ts asserts the `## work` section carries the Agent/in-session dispatch fork. Runtime state helpers live outside skills/ in src/kdream-helpers.ts.

name: KiloCode bridge companion (chat-panel relay)
user_story: As an orchestrator, I want to drive Kilo Code (which has no headless CLI) by relaying messages into its chat panel so that it participates in the fleet like CLI-driven hosts.
expected_behavior: A companion VS Code extension constructs a KiloCodeBridge on the comms dir, watches outboxes/kilocode/*.json + the ready flag, posts message text into Kilo's chat via executeCommand, writes processed/ audit records, and falls back to an OS toast (paste manually) if Kilo isn't installed.
gaps: The bridge-companion under adapters/ is README-only (no companion extension package.json/source in that folder); the actual bridge logic lives in src/bridge/kilocode.ts (403 lines) and is tested by bridge.test.ts
notes: adapters/kilocode also ships autoclaw-modes.yaml (generated customModes). KiloCode is the only host needing a bridge because it has no headless mode.

name: Launch Skill picker (host-aware prompt rendering)
user_story: As a developer, I want a 'Launch Skill' command that copies the right invocation for my active IDE so that I never get handed a file path that doesn't exist on my setup.
expected_behavior: autoclaw.launchSkill shows a two-step goal->action quick pick; resolves the active host, then renders a slash command for Claude Code or a 'Follow instructions in <dir>/<skill><ext>' pointer for rule-file hosts, copies it to clipboard, and offers to install adapters if the skill isn't present for that host.
notes: skillLauncher.ts is pure (no vscode/fs); SHIPPED_SKILLS launcher list = 6 skills (kdream, autobuild, mateam, orchestrate, security-auditor, doc-writer) and intentionally excludes intelligence even though intelligence has its own adapter. Codex host has no convention -> plain fallback instruction.

name: MAteam skill — multi-agent coordinator
user_story: As a developer, I want to spawn specialized sub-agents (Researcher->Coder->Reviewer->Verifier) working in parallel so that a complex task is decomposed across roles.
expected_behavior: The mateam skill detects whether the host exposes a literal `Agent` tool (Claude Code subagents) and dispatches each role as a separate Agent call, or forks in-session otherwise; it manages a per-session scratchpad and outputs a <=6-line report. Hard rule: never fabricate Agent calls.
notes: skills.test.ts is the dedicated gate: asserts 'Host detection & dispatch' header, Agent tool reference, in-session fallback, the 'do not fabricate' rule, and that it propagates to the regenerated cline adapter.

name: Orchestrate skill — parallel dev orchestrator
user_story: As a tech lead, I want an orchestrator that reads task manifests, builds dependency DAGs, generates sprints, and assigns scoped work so that parallel agents never collide on files.
expected_behavior: The orchestrate skill drives init/plan/assign/status/next/review/merge sub-commands against .autoclaw/orchestrator, enforces non-overlapping file scopes per sprint, and confirms in <=5 lines. Ships keepalive + starter + assignment templates for each host.
gaps: No test asserting orchestrate SKILL.md content or templates
notes: Largest skill: includes templates/ (keepalive per-host: claude-code, kilocode, kiro, cursor, codex, gemini-cli, etc.; starter bootstrap/coordinator/join/worker; review-checklist, sprint-assignment, completion-signal). Backing orchestrator logic lives in src/orchestrator/, outside this area.

name: Single-source skill authoring (SKILL.md)
user_story: As an AutoClaw maintainer, I want each skill defined once in skills/<name>/SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter so that every IDE adapter format is derived from one source of truth.
expected_behavior: Each skill is a markdown file with `---` frontmatter (name, description, optional trigger/tools/trust/provider) plus a body of operating rules. loadSkills() parses frontmatter and body; a missing-frontmatter file throws at build time.
notes: 11 SKILL.md files exist on disk (architect, autobuild, doc-writer, hermes, intelligence, kdream, loop-discipline, mateam, openclaw, orchestrate, security-auditor) but only 7 (SKILL_NAMES) feed the adapter generator. Frontmatter parser is shallow line-based, not a full YAML parser.

name: ZippyMesh LLM Router adapter (setup guide + playbooks)
user_story: As a developer running ZippyMesh, I want AutoClaw to detect it and drop an MCP setup guide and per-skill playbooks so that skills can route LLM calls through the local router.
expected_behavior: When a HEAD to autoclaw.kdream.zippymeshUrl succeeds during installAdapters, AutoClaw copies adapters/zippymesh/ (README setup guide + kdream/mateam/embeddings playbooks + mcp-setup.md) into .autoclaw/zippymesh/ and offers to open the guide.
gaps: Detection/copy path is not unit-tested; playbooks are static JSON, not generated
notes: Distinct from the skill adapter pipeline — these are static integration docs/playbooks copied on detection, not derived from SKILL.md.

name: Bi-temporal time-travel recall (factAsOf, timeline)
user_story: As a developer investigating regressions, I want to query what the system believed true on a past date using only knowledge recorded by then so that I can reconstruct historical state.
expected_behavior: factAsOf does a two-axis point query (validAt + knownAt, correctly hiding window-closes not yet known at knownAt); buildTimeline returns the full ordered per-subject history with isCurrent/validUntil; recall/query.ts wraps these as recallAsOf/recallTimeline/recallChain/recallCurrent.
gaps: recall.query MCP tool does NOT use this layer — it reads .autoclaw/dream/MEMORY.md and explicitly returns not_implemented for archive/as-of (tools.ts:646,635) | registerMemorySkills() (autoclaw.dream/autoclaw.recall commands) is a wiring stub never called from extension.ts; commands not in package.json
notes: Rich bi-temporal recall is implemented + tested but its only consumer is the dream register stub, not the live MCP surface.

name: Browser sub-agent capability flag + Playwright MCP fallback resolution
user_story: As an orchestrator dispatching a task that needs a browser, I want each runner's browser requirement resolved (native pass-through for Gemini, Playwright MCP for others) so that any agent can do web automation.
expected_behavior: needsBrowser/resolveBrowserProvision map (runnerId, needs_browser) to native | playwright-mcp | not-required; applyBrowserProvisionToMcp idempotently injects the @playwright/mcp server entry into a host's mcpServers map.
gaps: no test file for browserCapability | no production importer found — neither the dispatcher nor 'autoclaw mcp install' calls resolveBrowserProvision/applyBrowserProvisionToMcp
notes: C.13 pure resolver; appears unconsumed and untested.

name: Capsule replay + capture-from-actions
user_story: As a verifier confirming a fix, I want to replay only the failed acceptance gates from a stored capsule so that I can confirm the fix landed without re-running the whole review.
expected_behavior: replayFailedGates re-runs only the gates whose commands previously failed and reports pass/fail; captureFromChecks/captureCapsule mint a replayable capsule from a non-consensus run (failed autobuild, ingested CI, manual check), defaulting verdict from gate results.
gaps: replayFailedGates and captureFromChecks have no production caller (only buildCapsule path is wired); no command/MCP/HTTP route triggers a replay or a from-actions capture
notes: Implemented + tested (evidence.test.ts) but the replay/ingest surfaces are dormant seams.

name: Cross-project API dependency registry (consumer→producer edges, vendor-neutral)
user_story: As a producer about to change an API surface, I want to know which consumers in other repos pin that contract so that I notify exactly the affected consumers before breaking them.
expected_behavior: readDependencies/writeDependencies own ~/.autoclaw/programs/<id>/dependencies.json; consumersOf(producer, changedPaths) returns edges whose consumed_via globs intersect changed paths; addDependency is idempotent on (consumer,producer,api); backendFor resolves the abstract→concrete vendor-swap record; api field is always an abstract capability, vendors live only in backends.
gaps: imported only by program-dependencies.test.ts — no orchestrator/notification flow consumes consumersOf() yet; the §5.2 api_change notification is not wired
notes: MP-3/DR-1. Library complete + tested but no caller fires notifications.

name: Cross-repo comms tail (program-wide)
user_story: As an operator overseeing a multi-repo program, I want a single merged comms feed across all repos so that I can follow agent activity program-wide.
expected_behavior: tailCrossRepoComms reads each enabled repo's comms-log.jsonl, merges and chronologically sorts entries, applies a post-merge limit, and annotates each line with its source repo/label.
gaps: builder is unit-tested but has no command/panel/MCP consumer in production code (intended for the Fleet panel cross-repo tail, not wired)
notes: Library only; no production caller found.

name: Hierarchical memory tiers (core/recall/archive) + auto-promotion
user_story: As a memory system, I want facts to flow core→recall→archive over time within a strict core byte budget so that the always-loaded prompt context never overflows.
expected_behavior: planPromotions decides transitions (superseded→archive, core→recall after a dream cycle, recall→archive after N idle sessions); planCoreOverflow evicts largest-first when core exceeds 10KB; coreTierFits/factBytes police the budget; applyTransitions returns new records (pure).
gaps: pure planners are tested but no production scheduler applies transitions to on-disk tier files; the recall/index.json sidecar referenced in docs is not written by any wired code path found
notes: C3. Decision logic only — caller responsible for persistence, not located in production.

name: Per-persona sharded memory + privacy-gated global mirror
user_story: As a persona-based agent, I want my lessons sharded into scratch/recall/archive and only privacy-cleared lessons mirrored cross-project so that secrets and project-only knowledge never leak to global memory.
expected_behavior: appendLesson writes scratch JSONL; promoteLessons ages scratch→recall→archive (and archives superseded); mirrorToGlobal copies only isPromotableToGlobal entries (project entries never; secret-scan blocks even global) keyed by subject; writeDigest rebuilds lessons.md.
gaps: fully tested (personaMemory.test.ts) but no wired command/skill schedules promoteLessons/mirrorToGlobal in production; persona skills consume these via the persona memory layer but no extension-level scheduler found
notes: PA-1. Strong secret-scan + privacy gating; library complete.

name: Program-wide Agents table (single table with repo column)
user_story: As an operator, I want one agents table spanning every repo in the program so that I can see who is active/idle/stalled/offline across the whole fleet.
expected_behavior: buildProgramAgentsTable reads each repo's comms/registry.json roster + heartbeats/<agent>.json, derives a coarse status (active<2m, idle<5m, stalled<24h, offline) and returns a stable repo-then-agent sorted table.
gaps: no production consumer wires this builder into a panel/command/relay yet (D.4 rollup is the intended anchor)
notes: Pure data builder; tested only.

name: Reputation aggregation + routing factor (cold-start safe)
user_story: As a task router, I want a per-agent success multiplier (overall + per-capability) so that I can prefer agents that succeeded at similar tasks while never penalizing newcomers.
expected_behavior: aggregateReputation rolls outcomes into AgentReputation (samples/successes/success_rate, by_capability, avg_duration); reputationFactor maps success_rate∈[0,1]→[0.5,1.0], returns neutral 0.9 prior when <minSamples or unknown agent; getAgentReputation reads+aggregates one agent.
gaps: REP-2 routing is NOT wired: reputationFactor/getAgentReputation/aggregateReputation have no production consumer — no scorer/router calls them (grep shows only the ledger module + tests) | the Pro 'reputation-aware assignment' is advertised as a locked feature (extension.ts:1435)
notes: Aggregation logic complete + tested, but the consumer (router preference) is unbuilt; reputation data is recorded but not yet acted on.

name: Scope-lease primitive (program-plane, glob-scoped editing leases)
user_story: As an agent doing a long editing session, I want to hold a 30-min heartbeat-renewed lease over a path glob so that other agents do not concurrently edit the same files.
expected_behavior: acquireLease/renewLease/releaseLease maintain ~/.autoclaw/programs/<id>/leases.json; conflict = same project + intersecting globs + at least one exclusive; shared-read never conflicts; stale (heartbeat >2x interval) or expired leases are reclaimable; atomic tmp+rename writes; conservative glob-intersection.
gaps: module is imported only by program-leases.test.ts — NO production wiring (orchestrator/extension use the separate src/orchestrator/scopeLease.ts for file-scope leases instead)
notes: MP-2/LS-1 primitive. The wired 'AutoClaw: Declare File-Scope Lease' command uses orchestrator/scopeLease.ts, a different implementation — this program-plane lease is dormant.

name: Text recall over memory tiers (token-overlap)
user_story: As an agent, I want to retrieve relevant consolidated facts by free-text query without embeddings so that recall stays zero-LLM and local-first.
expected_behavior: recallQuery scores facts by fraction of query tokens matched in subject+content, filters by tier, excludes superseded facts by default, ranks by score then hotter tier then recency, returns topK.
gaps: the LIVE recall.query MCP tool (tools.ts) reimplements a separate token scan over dream/MEMORY.md rather than calling recallQuery over the bitemporal fact store — two divergent recall implementations | recallQuery itself has no command/MCP entry point
notes: MCP tool returns not_implemented when no MEMORY.md exists.

name: VoidSpec sync command + .voidspec/ watcher + dispatch
user_story: As a developer, I want AutoClaw to auto-resync when tasks.yaml changes and dispatch mirrored tasks so that VoidSpec edits flow into the orchestrator hands-free.
expected_behavior: watchVoidSpecDir watches .voidspec/ (chokidar with fs.watchFile polling fallback, debounced) and re-syncs on change; dispatchVoidSpecTasks hands mirrored tasks to a runner-voidspec if available, else converts them to native AutoClaw tasks; syncVoidSpecCommand returns a human summary.
gaps: VF-3 (BACKLOG): runner-voidspec dispatch runner not implemented (blocked on VoidSpec API) — dispatch always falls back to native conversion | watchVoidSpecDir is exported but the extension command only does a one-shot sync; no production caller auto-starts the watcher
notes: Native fallback path fully works; runner path is a seam only.

name: Workspace-local program scope registry (add/remove repos)
user_story: As a developer managing several sibling repos, I want to register them as one program so that AutoClaw treats them as a single fleet and can roll up status across them.
expected_behavior: addRepoToProgram/removeRepoFromProgram mutate .autoclaw/program/registry.json (a workspace-local JSON listing participating repos by absolute path); ensureProgramRegistry seeds the host workspace as the first member; idempotent, case-insensitive de-dup on Windows.
gaps: addRepoToProgramCommand() is a headless function with a TODO(extension.ts) to register the 'autoclaw.addRepoToProgram' command and contributes.commands entry — neither exists in package.json/extension.ts | the wired 'autoclaw.program.create/join/leave' commands use the DIFFERENT user-global program-plane.ts, not this registry

name: Bi-temporal fact schema + supersession chains
user_story: As an agent recording learned facts, I want append-only bi-temporal facts with supersession so that history is preserved and I can answer 'what did we believe about X as of date D'.
expected_behavior: createFact builds a fact with valid_from/recorded_at axes; supersede closes a predecessor (sets superseded_by + valid_to) returning fresh objects; resolveChain walks chains with cycle detection; currentFact returns the live head; never deletes.
notes: C4. Consumed by the /dream consolidation pipeline (skills/dream/pipeline.ts) and the recall query layer.

name: Budget ceiling (cost + wall-clock instrument with HALT enforcement)
user_story: As an operator running an autonomous fleet, I want a spend/time ceiling that halts the fleet when breached so that runaway agents can't burn money or time unbounded.
expected_behavior: checkBudget reads .autoclaw/orchestrator/budget.json, sums spend from the LLM cost ledger, measures wall-clock since an armed-at marker (survives restarts), and returns a queryable BudgetStatus; enforceBudget engages the fleet HALT switch once when a bound is breached; absent config ⇒ enabled:false no-op (opt-in).
notes: Wired into the orchestrator loop (orchestratorLoop.ts:656 calls enforceBudget); reuses CostLedger + fleetHalt. Opt-in via budget.json.

name: Evidence capsules (durable replayable run handles)

expected_behavior: buildCapsule (from a ConsensusResult) / captureCapsule (from a raw run) mint a stable run-<ts>-<hex> id and bundle gate recipe+results; writeCapsule persists atomically to consensus/results/; readCapsule/listCapsules fetch by handle/task; summarizeCapsule one-lines it.
notes: Wired: minted+written on every consensus evaluate (bridge.ts:568), surfaced via HTTP bridge /api/v1/capsules (bridge.ts:604) and boardWriter.ts:318.

name: Fact provenance (verified_by / 5-stage discipline)
user_story: As a memory reader, I want every fact to carry why it is believed so that a verified observation outranks an unverified guess.
expected_behavior: Provenance with method (session|tool_result|command|user|unverified); provenanceOf treats absent provenance as unverified; isVerified ranks facts; createFact leaves verified_by absent unless supplied to stay byte-compatible with pre-MEM-1 output.

name: Per-agent reputation ledger (track-record recording)

name: Intent-aware local/LAN/cloud model router (WL-2)
user_story: As a workflow author, I want model selection by intent + routing profile (cheap/balanced/quality/local-only/air-gapped/release-critical) with privacy localities, escalation on repeated failures, and a ZippyMesh recommendation hook, so that workflows pick the cheapest eligible local model first and only escalate to cloud when justified.
expected_behavior: routeWorkflowIntent() filters candidates by health, allowed locality (local-only/air-gapped force local; cloud reserved until escalation or quality/release-critical profile), cost ceiling, context window, and intent-derived capabilities; honors a recommendModel() override when its pick is eligible; falls back when no candidate has all capabilities (if allowed); scores survivors by benchmark/reputation/context/latency/profile and returns the selected model + rejected list + reason.
gaps: Standalone and unwired: nothing in runner.ts/state.ts/command.ts/nodes calls routeWorkflowIntent; the runner's agent node delegates routing entirely to whatever ModelProvider is injected (default mock). No real candidate-model source feeds it in production.
notes: Tested by src/test/workflow-intentRouter.test.ts. A complete, well-tested WL-2 routing brain that has no production caller yet.

name: Model/agent node executor seam
user_story: As a workflow author, I want an agent/model node that asks a pluggable ModelProvider for a completion and records a content-free decision (provider/model/locality/selectionReason/cost), so that model usage is auditable without leaking prompts.
expected_behavior: runModelNode() calls ctx.deps.modelProvider.complete with intent/profile/prompt/iteration, returns produced text + routing metadata as output and a content-free model decision + costCents. Default provider is the offline zero-cost defaultMockModelProvider (escalates mock-fast->mock-strong on iteration>=1).
gaps: NOT wired into the runner: runner.ts's 'agent' case (runner.ts:59) inlines its own modelProvider.complete call and never invokes runModelNode. So mockResponseText short-circuit and this module's exact output shape are not used in real runs.
notes: Tested by src/test/workflow-gateNode.test.ts. Real provider wiring (Ollama/LM Studio/ZippyMesh/cloud) is deferred to WL-2 per the file header; only the mock provider ships.

name: Tool node executor seam (mock/shell/test/mcp/action)
user_story: As a workflow author, I want a tool node seam that runs deterministic actions via an injectable CommandRunner, so that workflows can run shell/test/MCP/structured actions safely and mockably.
expected_behavior: runToolNode() handles kind 'mock' (returns mockOutput or simulates mockFailure) and routes shell/test/mcp/action through ctx.deps.commandRunner; missing command -> tool_format_invalid; timeout -> budget_exhausted; nonzero exit -> test_failure (kind 'test') or tool_action_illegal.
gaps: NOT wired into the runner: runner.ts implements its own inline tool executor (runner.ts:76-96) and never calls runToolNode. mcp/action kinds have no real MCP/adapter dispatch — they just go through CommandRunner like shell. | mockExitCode field declared but unused.
notes: Tested by src/test/workflow-gateNode.test.ts (shared file). A correct, tested seam that the production runner bypasses.

name: Workflow contract preflight validation
user_story: As an operator, I want a contract preflight that checks required tools/models/permissions are available and that no node writes to a no-touch invariant glob, so that a workflow is rejected before running if its declared capabilities or scope guarantees can't be met.
expected_behavior: validateWorkflowContract() requires a contract (else contract.missing), validates inputs/outputs/successCriteria non-empty with unique ids, checks required tools/models/permissions against an availability context emitting allow/deny PolicyDecisions, and flags write nodes whose target file matches an invariant noTouchGlob (scope_conflict). summarizeWorkflowContract returns counts/ids. globMatches supports * and **.
gaps: Not invoked by the runner — runWorkflow only calls validateWorkflow, not validateWorkflowContract, so contract/invariant/permission preflight is NOT enforced during an actual run. Only the tests harness and simulate consume it.
notes: Tested by src/test/workflow-contracts.test.ts. Solid preflight logic with no execution-time enforcement hook.

name: Workflow simulation / dry-run planner
user_story: As a workflow author, I want a dry-run that reports the likely execution path, blocked nodes, missing tools, eligible models, estimated cost/duration ranges, write-permission summary, and human-approval points, so that I can understand and de-risk a workflow before executing it.
expected_behavior: simulateWorkflow() runs validate + contract preflight, computes likelyExecutionPath (topo order), lists missing required tools, finds eligible models honoring routing-profile/privacy localities, summarizes write nodes and whether they need approval, surfaces policy blocks, and returns min/max cost+duration estimates scaled by node count and max iterations.
gaps: Pure library function with no command/MCP/CLI/UI entry point — no user-facing surface invokes simulateWorkflow. Only exported via workflows/index.ts and exercised by tests.
notes: Tested by src/test/workflow-simulate.test.ts. Cost/duration are crude linear heuristics (costPerNode default 1c, durationPerNode default 10s).

name: Workflow test harness (golden-path expectations)
user_story: As a workflow author, I want declarative test cases (inputs + mocks + expectations) that assert status/route/routing-profile/failure-type/artifacts/policy-decisions/node-output, so that I can regression-test workflow behavior deterministically.
expected_behavior: runWorkflowTestCase() validates+preflights, walks an indegree-based execution order applying node mocks or defaultNodeResult (input passes inputs through, gate uses mockPass/failureTypeOnFail, artifact emits a path), stops on first failed node, then evaluates each expectation and returns a WorkflowTestResult. formatWorkflowTestFailures renders human-readable failures.
gaps: This harness simulates execution with its own mock walker; it does NOT run the real runWorkflow engine, so the two execution semantics (e.g. loop handling, failure-edge routing) can diverge. No CLI/command exposes it. | loop nodes are not specially handled in its execution order.
notes: Tested by src/test/workflow-tests.test.ts. A separate evaluator from the runner — useful but not the same code path users actually run.

name: Bounded recursive loop executor (WL-1.2)
user_story: As a workflow author, I want loop nodes that re-run a body until a stop condition with hard budget/iteration/time ceilings and no-progress detection, so that self-correcting (generate-verify-revise, retry, best-of-N) loops can never run unbounded.
expected_behavior: runLoopNode() reads policy (maxIterations default 3, maxDepth, maxCostCents, maxWallTimeMs from config or node.budget), loops the injected body executor, exits 'completed' on body success, and halts with a typed reason on: HALT signal, cost ceiling, wall-time ceiling, same-failure-type repeated noProgressAfter times, unchanged output, human-required failure, non-retryable failure, or max iterations. Emits started/retrying/escalated/halted/human_required events with iteration index. resolveLoopBodyNodes derives the ordered body subgraph from edges (or trusts config.bodyNodes). loopStopReason maps to RunStopReason.
gaps: Loop config declares loop 'kind' patterns (retry / generate-verify-revise / retrieve-diagnose-reretrieve / best-of-N / mutation-test-strengthen) but readPolicy/runLoopNode do not branch on kind — all kinds run the same generic loop; the named patterns are documentation, not distinct behaviors. | config.stopOn / config.noProgress expression strings are declared in LoopNodeConfig but never evaluated.
notes: Tested by src/test/workflow-loops.test.ts. The runner integrates loops inline (runner.ts:362) re-deriving body ids via its own loopBodyIds rather than resolveLoopBodyNodes.

name: Built-in workflow fixtures (WL-0.5)
user_story: As a workflow author or UI, I want a set of small, valid example workflows (cheap-fix-loop, context-repair-loop, adversarial-test-loop, ...), so that I have working starting points and the UI/tests have representative graphs.

notes: Tested by src/test/workflow-fixtures.test.ts. Fixtures are validated only (parse + validateWorkflow), not executed in the fixtures test.

name: Failure taxonomy integration (diagnostics seam)
user_story: As the workflow engine, I want a single shared failure-type taxonomy with retryable/escalation/human-required classifiers, so that loops, gates, and routing make consistent retry/escalate/halt decisions.
expected_behavior: WorkflowFailureType aliases the diagnostics FailureType; state.ts re-exports isRetryableFailure/isEscalationCandidate/isHumanRequired/normalizeFailureType from src/diagnostics/failureTypes. Loops use isRetryableFailure/isHumanRequired to decide stop vs retry; the runner uses isHumanRequired to choose human_required vs node_failed stop reasons.
notes: Defined in src/diagnostics/failureTypes.ts (outside this area) but the workflow modules are the primary consumers. Covered indirectly by loops/gate/runner tests.

name: Gate node executor (deterministic judges) (WL-1.3)
user_story: As a workflow author, I want gate nodes (schema/compile/test/acceptance/budget/scope/mutation/review) that produce a typed pass/fail verdict mapped to the failure taxonomy, so that tools judge agent output and gate the downstream approve/artifact path.
expected_behavior: runGateNode() evaluates by kind: mockPass short-circuit; budget gate compares run.costCents to ceiling; schema gate checks requiredKeys on nearest upstream object; compile/test/acceptance/mutation/scope/review run config.command through the injectable CommandRunner and compare exit code to expectExitCode. Failures map to compile_error/test_failure/acceptance_failure/mutation_survived/artifact_invalid/scope_conflict/budget_exhausted/irreducible_or_needs_human. Returns verdict embedded in NodeExecResult output.gate.
notes: Tested by src/test/workflow-gateNode.test.ts. This is the ONE node module the runner actually imports and dispatches to (runner.ts:57).

name: Headless CLI runner (scripts/workflow-run.js) (WL-1.4)
user_story: As an operator or CI, I want a node CLI that runs a workflow file or the built-in cheap-fix-loop fixture fully offline, prints a content-free JSON summary, and exits nonzero unless completed, so that workflows can be driven from scripts/CI without the extension.
expected_behavior: node scripts/workflow-run.js --builtin cheap-fix-loop | --workflow <path> [--task "..."]: loads out/workflows/runner.js, builds defaultDeps with persistLedger and a deterministic mockCommandRunner (every command exit 0), runs the workflow, prints {runId,status,stopReason,failureType,cost,events,nodes,ledgerDir}, verifies the ledger via readRunEvents, and exits 0 only when status==='completed'. --builtin only accepts 'cheap-fix-loop'.
entry_points: CLI: node scripts/workflow-run.js --builtin cheap-fix-loop | CLI: --workflow <path> --task <task> | CLI flags: --builtin / --workflow / --task / --help
gaps: Requires a prior `npm run compile` (reads out/workflows/runner.js) or it fails with exit 3. | Only one built-in fixture; no CLI-level automated test (exercised manually as the WL-1 exit-gate demo).
notes: The cheap-fix-loop built-in here is a linear demo (input->agent->tool->gate->artifact), not an actual recursive loop node.

name: Headless workflow runner / DAG execution engine (WL-1.1)
user_story: As an agent or operator, I want to execute a validated workflow as a DAG of node executors headlessly, recording every node decision, so that workflows run deterministically and safely without surprise side effects.
expected_behavior: runWorkflow() validates, topologically orders non-loop nodes (Kahn with loop back-edge exclusion), executes nodes in order threading upstream outputs along edges, dispatches per node.type (gate/agent/tool/loop/human/passthrough), supports per-node retry, honors a cooperative shouldHalt signal before each node, routes failures through failure-path edges when present else halts, accumulates costCents, emits run events, and returns a RunResult with status/stopReason/failureType/nodeStates/events/ledgerDir. defaultDeps() supplies offline safe defaults.
gaps: The runner's agent and tool executors are inlined in runner.ts (executeNode) and do NOT call the dedicated src/workflows/nodes/modelNode.ts (runModelNode) or toolNode.ts (runToolNode); only runGateNode is wired. Those node modules are parallel, separately-tested seams that the runner doesn't use. | agent executor ignores node retry/escalation routing logic and intentRouter entirely.
notes: Tested by src/test/workflow-runner.test.ts. Safe-by-default: no shell or model call unless a CommandRunner/ModelProvider is injected.

name: JSONL run ledger — write/read/list/summarize with secret scrubbing (WL-0.4)
user_story: As an operator, I want every run's decision/cost events persisted to an append-only JSONL ledger plus a run.json summary, so that runs are auditable without ever storing prompt/response content.
expected_behavior: writeRunMetadata + appendRunEvent write to .autoclaw/workflows/runs/<runId>/{run.json,events.jsonl}. appendRunEvent scrubs keys containing prompt/response/secret/apikey/authorization/token (scrubSensitive). readRun/listRuns/summarizeRun read back, skipping corrupt/invalid lines with warnings, and compute cost/tokens/duration/failureTypes/status. A second simpler ledger (WorkflowRunLedger class + readRunEvents) lives in state.ts and is what the runner actually writes through.
entry_points: appendRunEvent() | writeRunMetadata() | readRun() | listRuns() | summarizeRun() | summarizeRunRecords() | WorkflowRunLedger (state.ts) | readRunEvents() (state.ts)
gaps: Two ledger implementations coexist: runLedger.ts (async, scrubbing, summaries) and state.ts WorkflowRunLedger (sync, no scrubbing). The runner uses the state.ts one, so events written during a real run are NOT passed through scrubSensitive — scrubbing only applies if a caller uses appendRunEvent directly.
notes: Same on-disk layout for both. Tested by src/test/workflow-runLedger.test.ts. Privacy scrub is real but only on the runLedger.ts path.

name: VS Code command: Workflow Lab — Run Workflow (WL-1.4)
user_story: As a developer, I want a VS Code command that lets me pick a *.workflow.json file from my workspace and run it headlessly with progress + an output channel, so that I can execute and inspect a workflow without leaving the editor.
expected_behavior: autoclaw.workflowLab.run prompts for a workspace folder if none, finds **/*.workflow.json (excluding node_modules, cap 50) via a QuickPick (or offers to open docs if none found), parses + validateWorkflow (shows up to 3 errors on failure), then runs via runWorkflow+defaultDeps(persistLedger:true) inside a cancellable withProgress (cancel sets shouldHalt), and prints status/stopReason/cost/events/ledger + per-node statuses to an 'AutoClaw — Workflow Lab' output channel plus an info toast.
gaps: No automated test for the command surface (VS Code command handlers are not in the unit suite). Uses default mock model provider + refusing command runner, so a picked workflow with real tool/gate commands will fail the command (exit 127) unless mock-configured — the command does not wire a real CommandRunner.
notes: Registered in extension.ts:1283. This is the only WL user-facing surface in the extension. Cancellation maps to a HALT signal.

name: Workflow DSL type model + parsers (WL-0.2)
user_story: As a workflow author, I want a typed JSON DSL (autoclaw.workflow.v1) with nodes, edges, contracts, policies, and run-event shapes, so that I can describe recursive agent workflows declaratively and tools can validate/run them.
expected_behavior: parseWorkflowDefinition/parseWorkflow accept a string or object and reject non-objects; stringifyWorkflowDefinition round-trips; rich interfaces (WorkflowDefinition, WorkflowNode, WorkflowEdge, WorkflowContract, WorkflowPolicies, WorkflowRunEvent, WorkflowRunState) define the full schema. Schema constants WORKFLOW_SCHEMA='autoclaw.workflow.v1' and WORKFLOW_RUN_EVENT_SCHEMA exported.
entry_points: parseWorkflow() | parseWorkflowDefinition() | parseWorkflowRunEvent() | stringifyWorkflowDefinition() | WORKFLOW_SCHEMA | src/workflows/index.ts re-exports
notes: Tested by src/test/workflow-types.test.ts. Two parallel type definitions exist: the full one in types.ts and a structural-subset shim in state.ts (the WL-0 integration seam documented at state.ts:5-29); they were intentionally kept separate to avoid a compile race but never collapsed.

name: Workflow graph validator (WL-0.3)
user_story: As a workflow author, I want a static validator that catches structural and config errors before a run, so that bad workflows fail fast with actionable diagnostics rather than at execution time.
expected_behavior: validateWorkflow() returns {valid, diagnostics[]} checking: schema string, required id/name/nodes/edges, duplicate node/edge/port ids, per-node-type config requirements (tool needs command/toolId/action; agent needs provider/model/mockComplete; gate needs criterion/check/command/mockPass), edge endpoints reference existing nodes/ports, policy budgets non-negative/positive, and detects cycles WITHOUT an explicit loop node (graph.cycle_without_loop). Each diagnostic carries a code, path, severity, and often a FailureType.
notes: Tested by src/test/workflow-validate.test.ts. Called by the runner, command surface, simulate, and tests harness.

name: Reputation-aware dispatch (BL-7)
user_story: As the orchestrator, I want work routed to the agent with the best track record so that proven agents get the work they are good at and repeat under-performers sink in the order
expected_behavior: dispatchPreferredByReputation reads the reputation ledger -> reputationByRunnerId -> getPreferred reputation criterion selects the highest-reputation enabled runner; newcomers get a neutral prior; empty ledger is a no-op
notes: First Phase-C wiring delivered. Hook caller still uses explicit runnerId by design; un-targeted dispatch entry point now exists + tested.

name: Production cost-ledger writer (BL-6)
user_story: As an operator, I want every LLM call recorded to the cost ledger so that budget ceilings, per-agent cost rollups, and fleet metrics actually have data
expected_behavior: LlmRegistry.chat() appends one sanitized LedgerRow per call (provider/model/operation/tokens/costCents/correlation ids; NO prompt/response content); failed calls counted with an error note; no provider reached = no phantom row. Lights up budget/ceiling, agentCost, fleetMetrics, intelligence ledgerBridge readers.
notes: Single production writer; readers were dormant for lack of data.

name: Config-driven loop-service runners registered (BL-14)
user_story: As an operator, I want loop_services[] from my AutoClaw config to become real dispatchable runners so that AutoGPT/LocalCoder/generic HTTP loop services join the fleet
expected_behavior: createDefaultRunnerRegistry now registers every runner from loopServiceRunnersFromConfig(opts.loopServices); empty/absent config = no extra runners (built-ins unchanged)
notes: Sonnet subagent; Opus-reviewed. Registry accepts+registers; extension.ts caller passing parsed config is the adoption follow-up.

name: VoidSpec YAML parser via js-yaml (BL-20)
user_story: As a user, I want VoidSpec task files with nested/multiline YAML to parse correctly so that real task manifests are not silently mangled
expected_behavior: parseVoidSpecYaml reimplemented on js-yaml load() preserving the exact return shape + validation; malformed input returns the same empty doc (no throw); nested/multiline/block-sequence now parse
notes: Sonnet subagent; Opus-reviewed. Watcher auto-start (VF-3, needs extension.ts) is a separate follow-up.

name: Recursive learning loop — closed + proven
user_story: As the system, I want routing to improve from observed task outcomes so that proven agents earn more work over time (reinforcement without training)
expected_behavior: End-to-end: recordTaskOutcome -> aggregateReputation/reputationFactor -> buildReputationPreference -> dispatchPreferredByReputation. Integration test proves the dispatch selection FLIPS after reversed outcomes are recorded (kilocode->claude-code).
notes: Loop substrate: BL-6 (cost) + outcomes recorded in bridge.ts/extension.ts via consensus + BL-7 routing. Open: invoke reputation-aware selection on an un-targeted production dispatch.

name: Capability-aware reputation routing
user_story: As the orchestrator, I want a task routed to the agent with the best track record FOR THAT capability so that security work goes to the proven security agent, tests to the proven tester, etc.
expected_behavior: buildCapabilityReputationPreference(ws,capability) + dispatchPreferredForCapability(registry,opts) scope reputation to a capability via reputationFactor(rep,capability); cold-start neutral prior; empty ledger no-op
notes: Sonnet subagent; Opus-reviewed 10/10. NEW files only (built during 3.6.9 commit). test:unit wiring DEFERRED until 3.6.9 lands.

name: Outcome fan-out → reputation + KG edge (A complete)
user_story: As the system, I want every reviewed task outcome to update BOTH the reputation ledger AND the knowledge graph so routing learns and the graph stays structurally true — from one event, not two pipelines
expected_behavior: Both production outcome sites (bridge.ts consensus-evaluate, extension.ts orchestrate review) now call recordOutcomeEdge after recordOutcomeOnce/recordTaskOutcome: one event -> {reputation row + KG edge (agent--completed-->task)}. Event-driven, best-effort, never blocks; NOT a 30s-tick poll.
notes: A co-design complete (KG agent owns recordOutcomeEdge @3.6.10; coordinator owns the 2 call sites). CI-safe: no SKILL.md/deps. 208 tests green. Ready to land as 3.6.11.

name: Reviewer capability roster (RF-1)
user_story: As the system, I want to scan which agents and models are available to review so that the tiered review router can pick the right reviewer for each gate
expected_behavior: buildReviewerRoster scans 3 planes (RunnerRegistry.detect, LlmRegistry, beacons w/ trust filter) -> ranked ReviewerCapacity[] {kind,locality,costTier,strength,healthy,reputation}; rankReviewers(tier) prefers local-first cheap for tier1 and strong+high-rep for tier2; degrade-safe; contract-independent.
notes: Sonnet subagent; Opus-reviewed 26/26. Contract-INDEPENDENT (no ScaffoldVariant/Score). RF-2/3 gated on codex OSL-1.1/2.1.

name: Tiered review router (RF-2)
user_story: As the system, I want a review routed gates-first to a cheap local checker and escalated to a strong cross-provider validator only when needed, so review is cheap by default and rigorous when it matters
expected_behavior: planReview(ReviewScaffoldConfig, roster, ctx) -> ReviewPlan: human-gate, panel (diversity-maximizing), tier1-local (cross-provider filtered), escalate to tier2-strong on high-stakes/tier1-gap; NEVER silently passes -> fail-safe humanRequired. Pure, consumes codex OSL-1.1 ReviewScaffoldConfig.
notes: Sonnet subagent; Opus-reviewed 30/30. Consumes OSL-1.1 contract (no shim). RF-3 service loop gated on codex OSL-2.1 scorer.

name: Scaffold scorer emits reward rows (OSL-2.1)
user_story: As the system, I want workflow run summaries and review verdicts converted into ScaffoldScore rows so scaffold routing can learn from verifier quality without training a model
expected_behavior: buildScaffoldScore() maps run status, gates, failure types, retries, cost, duration, review verdict, falseAccept/falseReject, and anti-hacking violations into bounded reward rows; scoreAndAppendScaffoldRun() appends sanitized JSONL rows through the OSL-1.1 store; run summaries now expose gate/retry counters.
notes: Codex-built. Anti-hacking violations force negative reward; corrupt/incomplete summaries degrade to warnings. RF-3 can now call the scorer to write ScaffoldScore feedback rows.

name: Review service core (RF-3)
user_story: As the system, I want a review request planned, dispatched, voted, and scored automatically so the review fleet churns through gates without a human in the inner loop — but never rubber-stamps
expected_behavior: processReviewRequest(input,deps): planReview -> dispatch reviewers best-effort -> conservative aggregate (any reject->reject) -> LABELED automated vote (automated:<scaffoldId>) -> emit ScaffoldScore (verifierPass/judgeVeto). Injectable IO. Invariants: never votes when humanRequired; empty verdicts FAIL-SAFE to human (never silent approve); crashed reviewer omitted not approved; scoring failure isolated.
notes: Sonnet subagent; Opus-reviewed 23/23. Consumes RF-1 roster + RF-2 router + OSL-1.1/2.1 contracts. Production wiring (real dispatch/voteWriter/scorer + watcher) = RF-4.

name: Review Fleet $0 validation gate (dry-run) + cross-provider independence fix
user_story: As the maintainer, I want the RF-1/2/3 loop proven end-to-end on the real device with zero paid-model spend before any paid validator is enabled, so the automated review fleet is safe to turn on
expected_behavior: scripts/review-fleet-dryrun.js scans the REAL device (detected 5 reviewers: claude-code + claude-desktop + zippymesh/ollama/lmstudio local-free), routes 5 representative scaffolds through processReviewRequest at $0, and asserts invariants (human-gate never votes; all-reviewers-crash fails safe to human; every vote labeled automated; 0-cent spend). Also fixed RF-2 reviewerProvider so claude-desktop/claude map to anthropic — cross-provider independence no longer slips a same-vendor reviewer through (tier1 now routes to the independent zippymesh LAN model).
notes: Dry-run PASS: 0 invariant violations, $0 spend; RF-2 30 tests green after the provider fix. Satisfies codex stale-consensus no-paid gate. Surfaced 92 stale consensus votes as archival candidates (finding, not auto-deleted).

name: Review Fleet production wiring RF-4a (dormant, off-by-default)
user_story: As the maintainer, I want the review fleet's dispatch/vote/score seams wired to real implementations but OFF by default, so I can enable automated reviewing with an explicit budget cap and never spend by accident
expected_behavior: defaultReviewFleetDeps(opts) returns a real ReviewFleetDeps: dispatchReviewer is gated behind enabled (default false) AND budgetCents (default 0) — $0 until explicitly enabled AND funded; parseVerdict is conservative (ambiguous/empty -> request_changes, NEVER approve); writeVote appends labeled automated votes to consensus/active with BOTH task_id and voter sanitized in the filename (path-traversal hardened); scoreRun calls scoreAndAppendScaffoldRun. Runner-kind dispatch stubbed for RF-4b. buildReviewPrompt is content-free.
notes: Sonnet-built, Opus-reviewed; review CAUGHT a path-traversal in writeVote (task_id was not sanitized) — fixed + 2 regression tests. 38 tests pass. Dormant: no paid model call until enabled+budget. Next: RF-4b runner dispatch + persistent watcher.

name: Review Fleet watcher loop RF-4b (dormant, off-by-default)
user_story: As the maintainer, I want a bounded watcher that automatically picks up pending review_requests and runs them through the fleet but stays off until I enable it, so reviews churn without a human in the inner loop
expected_behavior: startReviewFleetWatcher(opts): dormant gate FIRST (enabled default false -> returns immediately, never even scans); when enabled, bounded loop (maxCycles default 50) of runReviewFleetCycle with shouldStop HALT + injectable sleep; per-request error isolation (a throw increments errors + skips markProcessed so the msg retries, never aborts the cycle); built-in SAFE_DEFAULT_SCAFFOLD (tier1-local/different-provider/gatesFirst) prevents any silent approve.
notes: Sonnet-built, Opus-reviewed 16/16; full reviewfleet suite 133 green together. Dormant until enabled. RF-1..4b chain complete — only the enable flag + budget stand between here and a live fleet.

name: Board refresh: stale-item triage + reversible archive
user_story: As the maintainer, I want every stale vote/claim/board item validated as discardable or rework-with-priority before cleanup, so the board shows real signal without silently losing unfinished work
expected_behavior: Triaged 92 stale consensus votes + 56 claims against sprint status + landed tests + live code + canonical CSV; archived 94 votes + 43 claims (reversible, with manifests) to _archive/2026-06-29; kept 3 active votes + live WL/ORNITH claims. Validation CAUGHT the flagship BL-7 reputation-dispatch as built-but-UNWIRED (REWORK/HIGH, not discard) + 2 panel items as unmerged-branch rework. Board now: 3 active votes, 0 stale.
notes: Reversible (move not delete + manifest per archive). REWORK items appended to GAP-ANALYSIS. Driven by maintainer directive: validate before discard.

name: BL-7a: reputation-preferred dispatch wired into the live hook path
user_story: As the system, I want a trigger hook with no explicit target to wake the best runner by reputation instead of erroring, so the flagship reputation-aware routing actually fires in production
expected_behavior: extension.ts spawnRunner hook: when target is empty/auto/best, the direct-dispatch path now calls dispatchPreferredByReputation(registry,{workspaceRoot}) (no explicit runnerId) so getPreferred's §5.5 reputation criterion finally decides; explicit-target wakes unchanged; still behind AUTOCLAW_RUNNER_DIRECT_DISPATCH process-launch gate. The reputation ENGINE was built+tested (F-325/329) but called nowhere — this is its first live production caller.
notes: Full tsc -p clean; engine proven by runner-reputation-wiring.test (routes to higher-rep runner). REACH IS LIMITED to the hook direct-dispatch path; BL-7b = wire reputation into the orchestrator default assignToVendor selection (behavior change, needs design nod).

name: RF-4c: Review Fleet inbox adapter (watcher <-> real comms tree)
user_story: As the maintainer, I want the watcher to read real review_request messages and mark them processed so the fleet can run against the live comms tree once enabled
expected_behavior: src/reviewfleet/inbox.ts: scanReviewRequests reads inboxes/shared + agent inbox for type=review_request not already in processed/ (dedup by content id), tolerates malformed files; markReviewRequestProcessed atomically renames the matched file into processed/ (idempotent); defaultReviewFleetWatcherDeps is the one-call factory wiring scan+markProcessed+defaultReviewFleetDeps. Dormant inherited (enabled=false).
notes: Sonnet-built, Opus-reviewed 20/20; no path-traversal (moves existing dir entries by content-id match). RF-1..4c complete — the fleet can now run against the real tree the moment a caller passes enabled:true + budget.
